post.description:
Re-run daily digests for a targeted list of trips.

Designed for outage recovery: when the background digest loop logs
``failed_trip_ids``, paste those IDs here to re-run without waiting
for the next 6-hour cycle.

Each trip's ``day_number`` is computed from its ``start_date`` and
the target date.  Trips with no ``start_date``, or where the target
date falls outside ``[start_date, end_date]``, are skipped with
status ``no_date``.

The endpoint is idempotent — ``generate_daily_digest`` respects its
input-hash skip optimisation, so re-running a trip that already has
an up-to-date digest returns ``status="skipped"`` without billable LLM
calls.

get.description:

Useful for evaluating how well the triage agent is targeting notifications

are a signal that the LLM is over-notifying or picking the wrong channel.

Returns:
- Overall response rate across all recorded outcomes
- Average response window in minutes for outcomes that received a reply
- Per-urgency breakdown (response rate by urgency tier)
- Per-outcome distribution (achieved / not_achieved / pending / …)
- Top-10 intents sorted by response count

Freshness of the catalog facet index (``entity_facets``).

The nightly ``project_catalog_facets`` cron stamps ``updated_at = now()`` on
every row. If the index goes empty or stale (the cron stopped running),
Discover/Atlas compose silently degrade to world-only personalization with
no other signal — so poll this and alert on ``stale``. ``stale`` is True when
the index is empty or older than the staleness threshold (>48h = ≥2 missed
nightly runs).

Owner-edit the shareable artifact's one-line read (item 2 / R11).

Makes the postcard headline correctable rather than delete-only. Editing
flips ``composed_by`` to ``"user"`` so the honesty badge reads "edited by

post.description:
Parse a natural-language taste query into known facets + an honest residual.

Maps the query to ONLY the live dimension vocabulary
(cuisine / category / neighborhood / experience_type / genre); each recognized
facet's ``dimension``+``value`` are directly usable as ``GET /api/atlas/board``
steer params. Anything the engine has no dimension for (weather, companion,
mood, free text) is left verbatim in ``residual_phrase`` — never forced into a
fabricated dimension.

LLM-backed when credits are available; degrades to a deterministic keyword
match against the traveler's known loved facet values otherwise. Never raises.

Save (keep) a composed board so the traveler can re-open it later.

The saved row is just the query: raw ask + facet_set + residual phrase, plus
a cached display title. When ``title`` is omitted the board is recomposed
once to cache its real title. Idempotent — saving the same query twice (and
a re-save of a previously-dismissed one) returns the same row id.

get.description:
List the traveler's non-dismissed kept boards, newest first.

Each board carries its ``facet_set`` (re-open recomposes live via

Back In ``freshness`` state (live/updated/active/thin — canon §6),
computed fresh on every list call.

post.description:
Snapshot this kept board's current moments as "seen" — the baseline
the NEXT ``GET /api/atlas/boards`` freshness diff runs against.

Call this the moment a kept row is tapped open, fire-and-forget (never
block navigation on it). Recomposes once server-side so the FE doesn't
need to know/pass moment ids. Idempotent from the client's view: an

get.description:
List the traveler's strongest composable facets for the compose surface.

Recognition-first: aggregates ``get_loved_facets`` across the projected
dimensions (cuisine / experience_type / neighborhood / genre) plus
``get_loved_categories`` as the ``category`` dimension, ranks by loved-entity
count (weight as tie-break), dedupes on ``(dimension, value)``, and caps to a
handful. Each suggestion's ``dimension``+``value`` are the exact steer params
for ``GET /api/atlas/board`` — a tap composes that board directly.

Grounded only: every facet here is one the affinity substrate already carries;
an empty substrate yields an empty list (no fabrication).
Paginated postcard/artifact shelf for Atlas.

Search Atlas-owned memories, pending candidates, and Travel DNA phrases.

The artifact leg uses the same Qdrant hybrid (dense + BM25) lane Universal
Search's Atlas group already queries — see
``backend.core.db.atlas.async_search_artifacts_semantic`` — gated behind
``ATLAS_SEMANTIC_READ``; off (or on Qdrant/embedding failure), it degrades
to the same pg_trgm-scored keyword match this endpoint always used, so
prod behavior is unchanged until the flag flips. The candidates leg stays
plain SQL (candidates aren't embedded) and the phrase-suggestion leg's
in-memory match is untouched.

post.description:
Approve every pending candidate in one shot.

Each candidate is processed with the same atomic claim-first pattern as
the individual approve endpoint, so concurrent calls are safe. Candidates
that fail composition are skipped (claim reverted); the caller gets a
partial result. Always uses the mock composer — running N sequential LLM
composes inside a single request risks the idle-timeout and produces
~60–120 s latency; the individual approve path uses the LLM.

Submit one or more client-clustered candidates for the user to review.

Backend validates each candidate's invariants (date order, photo count,
confidence tier matches stats) and persists. Rejects the whole batch
on any invalid candidate to keep client retry logic simple.

delete.description:
Mark a candidate as dismissed. Idempotent — repeated calls succeed silently.

Dismissed candidates do not re-appear in subsequent scans (the client
is expected to check the dismiss status before re-submitting candidates
that match a previously-dismissed date+place cluster).

``async_dismiss_candidate`` only writes from ``pending`` state (see its
docstring) and returns False otherwise — e.g. the candidate is already
``approved``, or a concurrent request dismissed/approved it between the
read above and this write. That return value used to be discarded: the
route fired the "dismissed" telemetry event and returned 204 regardless,
even when the write never happened.

post.description:
Approve a candidate and kick off artifact generation.

Pass ``{"style": "postcard"}`` in the request body to select a
narrative voice. Omit the body entirely for the default Memory style.

Uses claim-first ordering (atomic UPDATE WHERE status='pending') to
eliminate the TOCTOU race from the old read-check-then-act pattern.
Double-tap returns the existing artifact idempotently.

Persists with ``state="kept"`` — approving IS keeping, same as keep-all
(B1, 2026-07-06). ``state`` is the user's intent (did they keep it);
``composed_by``/``generation_status`` track authorship/compose progress
separately and are orthogonal — a kept artifact can start as mock copy
and be upgraded to LLM prose in place without ever changing ``state``.

delete.description:
Delete an artifact and cascade-revoke its derived signals.

Triggers ``refresh_personal_memory()`` afterward so the next Travel DNA
synthesis sees the reduced signal set. Eventual-consistency note: the
LLM aggregate may preserve a signal it can no longer justify; the
dispute mechanism remains the user-facing fallback for stuck signals.
See the V0 plan's Signal extraction section.

get.description:
SSE stream of artifact generation events.

Two-phase: a ``shell`` event first (title / hero / map / placeholders
so the UI has something to render immediately), then a ``ready`` event
with the full artifact. True section-by-section streaming (yielding
sections as the LLM produces them) is V1 work — it needs the composer
to switch to ``stream_text`` and parse JSON incrementally. Today the
composer is single-shot, so this endpoint reads from the persisted
artifact and emits the two-phase shape for UI parity.

Toggle the artifact's signal state.

Uses the project's preferred PATCH idiom (``model_fields_set``) — only
fields the client explicitly set propagate to the DB. See
``.claude/rules/patch-routes.md``.

patch.description: Keep, dispute, or forget one Atlas-derived signal.

Moment Actions "This isn't right" — dispute/forget/re-activate one
composed moment.

A Moment's id IS its source ``traveler_place_affinity.entity_id`` (see
``backend.core.models.composition.Moment``), a different id space from the
derived-signal dispute above — this writes to the affinity row directly.

C3 (2026-07-06): the read-side effect is now GLOBAL, not Atlas-only — the
shared reader (``get_loved_places``, and everything built on it:
``get_loved_facets``/``get_loved_categories``, plus ``get_intended_
destination`` and ``get_affinity_labels``) filters ``user_state='active'``
at the SQL layer, so Discover recs, home nudges, and any other consumer of
this table stop surfacing the disputed/forgotten place too. Before this
fix, the suppression was Atlas-composition-only (``taste_board._drop_
disputed``) and every other subsystem still read the raw row.

Persist a real artifact photo: multipart bytes → CDN (rehosted).

Replaces the old presigned-URL stub (it returned ``example.invalid``).
Reuses the working media pipeline (``rehost_from_bytes`` → EXIF/variants/CDN,
the same path trip photos use). When ``artifact_id`` is supplied and owned by
the caller, the CDN url is stamped as the artifact's ``hero_photo_url`` — the
source the img2img postcard render reads.

Rehost one photo from a KEPT artifact to a durable CDN url.

``source_photo_id`` is the on-device PHAsset id (the same id the
artifact's ``photo_ids``/``hero_photo_id`` already carry) — the client
resolves it to bytes locally (e.g. via expo-media-library) and posts
them here. Idempotent: re-uploading the same ``source_photo_id`` for
the same artifact replaces the existing rehost rather than duplicating.
404s for an artifact the caller doesn't own — never a hint about
another user's artifacts existing.

get.description:
The rehosted-photo map for a kept artifact: source PHAsset id -> CDN url.

A photo absent from ``urls`` has no durable fallback yet — the client
keeps rendering it via ``ph://`` (with D4's honest-unavailable state as
the final fallback). 404s for an artifact the caller doesn't own.

post.description:
Render an artifact's uploaded hero photo into a riso postcard (img2img).

Reads the artifact's CDN ``hero_photo_url`` (set by the upload route) as the
source, applies the flag → daily-cap → quality-floor guard rails in
``render_postcard``, and on success stamps ``rendered_image_url`` onto the
artifact (the postcards shelf prefers it). SHIPS DARK: with the feature flag
off this returns ``feature_disabled`` without ever calling a provider.

get.description:
Flat chronological timeline of a user's travel life.

Returns trips (planned + taken), pending Atlas diary drafts, and
kept Atlas memories in one normalised contract, sorted by
``occurred_start`` descending.

Pass ``year=YYYY`` to restrict to entries whose date range overlaps
that year, or ``undated=true`` for entries with no ``occurred_start``
at all (D2, 2026-07-06 — Long View's "Undated" chapter; previously
unreachable since ``year=0`` 422'd). Mutually exclusive: ``undated=true``
ignores any ``year`` also passed. Omit both to get the full timeline
(capped at ``limit``, default 100).

Pagination uses keyset cursors (entry UUIDs). Each page may fetch
extra rows server-side so deduplicated pages still return up to
``limit`` visible entries.

V1 reads from the persisted ``atlas_timeline_entries`` projection,
lazily backfilled on first access for legacy users.

Send a message to the concierge for this trip.

Delegates to the DB-backed conversation session. Creates the trip's
group conversation on first call.

Optional ``X-Idempotency-Key`` header deduplicates retries: a second
call with the same key returns the cached response (if the original
completed) or 409 Conflict (if still processing).

Send a message and stream the response via Server-Sent Events.

Streams text deltas as they arrive from the LLM, then sends a final
event with metadata (tool calls, token counts, etc.).

Event types:
    - ``text_delta``: partial text chunk (``{"text": "..."}``).
    - ``metadata``: final turn metadata, sent once after the reply completes.
    - ``error``: if an exception occurs during processing.

Optional ``X-Idempotency-Key`` header deduplicates retries. A repeat
request with the same key whose original is still streaming emits an
``error`` SSE event with ``kind="in_flight"``; a repeat whose original
completed emits the cached reply as ``text_delta`` followed by metadata.

Optional ``X-Idempotency-Key`` header deduplicates both human-only sends
and agent turns. Human messaging must tolerate lost-response mobile retries
without inserting the same group message twice.

post.description:
Send a message and stream the response via Server-Sent Events.

Streams text deltas as they arrive from the LLM, then sends a final
event with metadata (tool calls, token counts, etc.).

If ``trigger_agent`` is False, returns a single SSE event with the
persisted message ID (no streaming needed).

Event types:
    - ``text_delta``: partial text chunk (``{"text": "..."}``).
    - ``metadata``: final turn metadata, sent once after the reply completes.
    - ``user_message``: when trigger_agent=False, the persisted message.
    - ``error``: if an exception occurs during processing.

Optional ``X-Idempotency-Key`` header deduplicates retries. A repeat
request with the same key whose original is still streaming emits an
``error`` SSE event with ``kind="in_flight"``; a repeat whose original
completed emits the cached reply as ``text_delta`` followed by metadata.
Cancel this actor's currently running turn without treating socket loss as cancellation.

Stamp the conversation's ``last_location`` with a fresh reading.

Participants only — the conversation's GPS is private to its
members. Mobile clients piggyback location on message sends for
the common path; this endpoint exists for background pushes
(foreground event, significant-change updates) where there's no
message to attach to.

Idempotent-ish: latest write wins. Readings older than 15 minutes
are filtered at read time by the origin resolver, so stale pushes
don't pollute — but callers shouldn't push stale readings anyway.

Create the trip from the exact proposal version the user reviewed.

The proposal row, trip row, conversation link, membership copy, and
structured-message receipt are committed in one transaction. Repeating a
successful confirmation returns the same trip. A stale version returns 409
with the current version so the client can refresh before asking again.

Promote a pre-trip conversation into a committed trip.

Reads the conversation's ``intent_state`` scratchpad, creates a trip
row with best-effort field mapping (title / place_id / dates),
links them both ways, copies conversation participants as trip
members, and stamps ``intent_state.phase='committed'``.

Only the conversation creator may promote — the resulting trip is
owned by that user (``trip.created_by = conversation.created_by``)
so allowing anyone else to drive this would let an attacker spawn
trips under a victim's name. Normal participant auth is not strong
enough here because trip creation is an ownership-changing action.

Fails with:
- 403 if the caller isn't the conversation creator
- 400 on not-found / home / already-promoted / not-ready

B6 audit fix: settlement is now restricted to either:
  * The payer (``actor.id == expense.paid_by``) — confirming "yes,
    these members paid me back", OR
  * Self-declaration (``body.user_ids == [actor.id]``) — declaring
    "I paid the payer back for my share".

Previously any trip member could mark ANY other member's share as
settled, falsely zeroing out a debt and effectively defrauding the
payer (the settlement screen showed the debt as cleared; the payer
never got paid).

Settle multiple expense shares in one request.

The single-expense ``/expenses/{id}/settle`` endpoint is unchanged;
this batch path collapses what was N parallel POSTs in
``Travel App/app/trip-expenses/index.tsx`` (one per expense in a
payer column) into one round-trip. Authorization is enforced
per-item — the same payer-or-self-only gate as the single endpoint
runs for every batch member, so a malicious caller cannot piggyback
a "settle someone else's debt" on top of a legitimate item.

Behaviour: fail-fast on the first unauthorized or missing item; no
partial commits. On success the response carries every updated
expense in request order so the client can refresh its cache in one
pass.

Record a real-world transfer between two travelers and return the
freshly recomputed trip settlement.

2026-07-14: this is the fix for the trip-wide "Settle up" flow, which
used to guess which expense shares corresponded to a net (possibly
chained/simplified) transfer amount and flip is_settled on them —
sometimes the wrong ones, corrupting the balance graph. Recording the
actual transfer instead of guessing a share mapping is unambiguous by
construction: the graph nets exactly the amount that changed hands, no
matter how many expenses or people it was originally chained through.

Either side of the transfer may record it — the person who paid
(self-declaring "I paid X"), or the person who received it (confirming
"X paid me") — mirroring the existing single-share settle endpoint's
payer-or-self-only authorization (B6).

are included (rendered as voided by the client): history stays honest.

post.description:
Speculative pre-render hint — fires LLM + TTS before geofence dwell expires.

Phase Voice-1 / Phase 2.4. Called by the client when the user is
approaching a known stop (within ~200 m and walking toward it).
The endpoint is a thin wrapper that:

1. Verifies the actor is a trip member (existing auth).
2. Verifies the requested stop is part of THIS trip (avoids
   arbitrary speculative renders for stops the user isn't on).
3. Checks if audio for this stop is already cached → no-op return.
4. Otherwise enqueues ``render_intros_for_user_trip`` for the
   actor (the trip-level fan-out is idempotent; the worker skips
   already-rendered stops).

Idempotent and racy-safe — multiple clients triggering the same
hint within the cache TTL all converge on the same cached audio,
so over-firing is cheap. The endpoint accepts a single
``(entity_type, entity_id)`` rather than a list to keep the client
simple; if the user is approaching multiple stops, the client
fires multiple hints in parallel.

get.description:
Return the offline-narration manifest for the actor on this trip.

Phase Voice-1 / Phase 2.5. The client fetches this once on WiFi
pre-departure, batch-downloads every audio entry to local storage,
and plays from the local path while offline / on expensive data.

Each ``audio_url`` is relative to the API base — clients construct
the absolute URL via their own configured BASE_URL. Re-fetching the
manifest after additional renders complete returns a superset of
the previous response (idempotent + monotonic).

The ``ready_count`` and ``pending_count`` let the client surface a
"ready for offline" indicator. When ``pending_count > 0``, the
client may choose to defer the offline-bundle download and re-poll
the manifest after a short delay.

head.description:
HEAD probe for pre-rendered narration audio — cheap existence check.

Returns 200 with ``Content-Length`` and ``Content-Type`` headers when audio
is cached and ready, 404 when not.

Why a dedicated HEAD route rather than relying on FastAPI/Starlette's
implicit HEAD-from-GET behaviour:
- The GET handler returns a ``StreamingResponse`` backed by a generator.
  Starlette's implicit HEAD strips the body but still executes the handler
  including the generator, meaning it fetches the full audio blob just to
  discard it.
- This explicit HEAD issues a single lightweight COUNT-style query against
  ``narration_audio_cache``, reading only ``audio_size_bytes`` (no blob).
- Matches the client contract: ``useNarrationAudio`` does a HEAD check
  to confirm availability before creating an ``expo-audio`` player;
  eliminating the GET overhead makes that check ~10× cheaper.

get.description:
Return pre-rendered MP3 audio for a stop, or 404 if not yet cached.

Used by ``useNarrationAudio`` on the mobile client to play narration audio
when available. The audio cache is populated by the prerender pipeline
(``backend/guide/prerender.py``) which fires on ``itinerary.committed``
events. Cache miss is normal — the chat narration card silently
omits the play button when audio isn't ready.

For a lightweight existence check (no blob transfer), use the sibling
``HEAD /audio/{entity_type}/{entity_id}`` endpoint.

Enqueues a background composition job and returns 202 Accepted
immediately. The client should poll GET /api/trips/{trip_id}/story
until it receives a 200 with the full TripStory body.  Using a
background job prevents this POST from blocking for the 30–90s
that a Sonnet recomposition takes.

The job is deduped via ``_job_id`` so rapid taps never spawn more
than one concurrent recompose run per user.

returns **202 Accepted** with ``{"status": "composing"}``.  The
client should poll every few seconds until it receives a 200 with the
full TripStory body.  Using a background job (instead of an inline
``compose_trip_story`` call) keeps this GET from blocking for the
30–90s that a Sonnet composition takes on slow networks.

The job is deduped via ``_job_id=f"story:{trip_id}:{user_id}"`` so
a polling client never spawns more than one composition run per user.

Return the cached cross-trip threads for ``trip_id``.

Each thread carries a short concierge-voice prose string plus 1-2
references to prior trips (hydrated with title at read time so the
frontend doesn't need a second fetch per chip).

Behaviour:
- Cache miss → schedule background recompute, return empty threads.
- Cache stale (source_trip_count < current completed-trip count for
  this user) → schedule background recompute, return cached threads

- Cache fresh → return cached threads directly.

No threads is the canonical state for users with < 2 prior completed
trips. The frontend hides the card when threads is empty.

get.description:
Cheap poll endpoint: is a group turn with Vesper in flight right now?

Backed by a short-TTL shared cache entry (backend/core/group_presence.py),
not a database read — this is meant to be polled every few seconds from
the group chat screen. Returns ``active=False`` on any miss (no turn in
flight, cache unavailable, or malformed entry) — never raises, since a
presence failure must never break the chat screen.

Tier 1 group live-presence (docs/working/Chat Waiting Experience
Plan.md, Workstream A) — read-only SSE observer stream.

Other group members open this to see the SAME progress events
(thinking/tool_started/tool_complete/step_started) the turn owner's own
stream gets — never the reply's actual text_delta tokens (see
backend/core/group_turn_broadcast.py's module doc for why). This is an
additive enhancement on top of Tier 0's poll (still the correct
"who's asking" answer even if this stream drops); a client that never
opens it, or whose Redis is unconfigured, only loses the live-progress
detail, never the underlying feature.

Push durable group-history invalidations to authenticated members.

Frames contain identifiers only. Clients refetch the canonical message
endpoint, so sender attribution, visibility filtering, and card hydration
stay centralized there. Missing Redis simply closes this enhancement; the
client continues polling.

Pre-flight conflict check for the Move sheet's IF YOU MOVE IT section.

Returns:
- ``overlaps`` — hard overlaps on the target day (Phase 2)
- ``travel_*_ok`` — soft travel-time warnings against adjacent blocks
- ``would_create`` — Phase 3 standing-watcher scan run against the target
  day with the prospective move applied in-memory; surfaces tight_hop,
  over_packed, dead_gap, budget_overrun BEFORE the user commits (X-CRIT-2)

Nothing is written to the DB. The frontend calls this debounced as the
user adjusts time/day.

Return 3–5 suggested time windows for a block (BETTER SLOTS, §5.2).

Scans the block's day (or ``target_day_id`` for cross-day move previews)
for clear gaps; labels each by time of day; estimates walk time.

Gap-aware venue suggestions for the Add sheet's Suggestions tab.

Searches venues near the midpoint of the adjacent blocks' venues (or the
trip's destination centroid when no neighbours have coordinates). Ranked by
time-of-day appropriateness, proximity, then price. Returns the same
``BlockAlternativesResponse`` shape as ``/alternatives`` so the frontend
can reuse the candidate card component.

Scan all days for §7.2 planning conflicts (standing watcher).

Returns conflicts grouped by day. Clients call this on plan open and
after any itinerary edit. No edit-mode gate — read-only.

patch.description:
Set or clear the trip's daily / total budget.

Organizer-only. Passing ``null`` for a field clears it; omitting a field
leaves it untouched. (PATCH semantics — distinguished via
``model_fields_set`` so a request that only sets the daily budget does
not silently null out the total budget.) Amounts are in the smallest
currency unit (cents for USD/EUR).

post.description: Prepare one atomic optimize-day operation and its human-readable diff.

get.description:
The in-app leave-by hint for this user + trip's imminent next stop.

Computed fresh (the READ twin of the push dispatcher), so it works even for
users with push disabled — the whole point of surfacing it in-app. Returns
``{"hint": null}`` when there's no qualifying stop or the user muted/dismissed
it; the client hides the strip rather than showing an empty one.

post.description:
Record that the acting user dismissed the leave-by nudge for a block.

Sets ``dismissed_at`` on the most-recent leave_by_dispatches row for
(trip_id, block_id, user_id).  Idempotent — a second dismiss has no
visible effect.  Suppression rule 2 in the dispatcher checks this field.

Mute all leave-by nudges for this user + trip for the rest of today.

Inserts a leave_by_mutes row keyed on (user_id, trip_id, date). The date
is taken from ``body.local_date`` when provided (the device's local calendar
date), falling back to today in UTC. Using the device date prevents timezone
skew for users ahead of UTC (e.g. UTC+9) who would otherwise mute the wrong
day.

Suppression rule 1 in the dispatcher checks this table before dispatching.
Idempotent — inserting the same mute row twice is a no-op (composite PK).

Nudge one or more trip members about a stay vote or a settle-up.

Any trip member can nudge any other trip member — this is peer
coordination, not an organizer power. Each target is processed
independently (one cooled-down or gated target doesn't block the others
in the same request); see ``NudgeTargetResult.skipped_reason`` for why a
given target didn't get a fresh send.

Aggregates each trip's itinerary blocks that resolved to coordinates into
a single list tagged by trip, for the Memory-surface map (everywhere
you've been, pins colored by trip). The per-trip equivalent is

delete.description:

Irreversible. Shared trips and group conversations the user created are

photos, bookings, devices, notifications — is removed. See
``backend.core.db.account_deletion.delete_user_data`` for the full
cascade and the cooperatively-owned-data policy.

The home conversation is the trip-less chat where the concierge

This endpoint also backfills for any user who predates the bootstrap.

delete.description:

post.description:
Set or change the user's phone number.

Clears any prior verification state — changing the number requires
a fresh verification before SMS will be delivered. Pass
``phone_number=null`` to remove the number entirely.

Idempotency: a stable ``X-Idempotency-Key`` returns the cached
response (bad-network audit 2026-05-19). Without it, a retry races
the unique partial index and either returns ``409`` (number now
"in use" by the user's own re-write) or silently succeeds with a
different verification state than the original request observed.

Generate a fresh OTP and send it via SMS.

Subsequent calls within the 10-minute expiry invalidate the prior
code (fresh OTP). No rate limit beyond what Twilio enforces —
worth adding server-side throttling when real usage warrants.

Idempotency: critical here because a flaky-network retry on the
"Send code" button would otherwise burn a second SMS against the
user's per-number rate-limit budget (and produce a confusing UX
where two codes land seconds apart). The cache returns the prior
response (provider + sent flag) so the retry is a no-op.

Submit the OTP to mark the phone number as verified.

Idempotency: a retry of the same code submission returns the
cached response rather than burning a second attempt against the
user's rate-limit budget.

Record a geofence trigger and (if applicable) open a session.

Auto-session semantics:
  - On ``entered`` AND no active session → open one anchored at the
    triggering entity and bind the new event row to it via session_id.
  - On ``entered`` for a different entity → open a new session. The
    CRUD helper closes the prior open row with ``next_place`` first,
    preserving its arc summary/history while starting a fresh arc.
  - On any other event type, or when the entered entity matches the
    active session → record with the current session_id (when present).

The auto-open behavior keeps the client flow tight: a fresh geofence
enter → one HTTP call → client knows both the event id and the
session id. Saves the round-trip vs separate "create session" + "record event."

get.description: Basic liveness check — always returns 200 if the process is running.

Readiness check — Postgres gates routing; Qdrant is reported, not gated.

Postgres is a hard dependency for nearly every endpoint (auth, trips,
chat persistence, ...), so an outage genuinely means "this machine
can't serve traffic" — Fly should mark it unhealthy and restart.

Qdrant is NOT: only the vector-search-touching paths need it, and
most of the app (auth, trip management, most of chat) works fine
without it. Pre-fix, a Qdrant outage 503'd this whole check, so Fly
cycled every machine and took down the entire API over a dependency
only some endpoints actually need. Qdrant's status is still surfaced
in the response body for dashboards/alerting — it just doesn't gate
the HTTP status.

Monitoring-only check of external API reachability (B7).

Returns status of outbound dependencies the runtime relies on:
Anthropic (LLM), Tavily (web search), and Redis (the arq worker's
job intake). Used by dashboards / alerting — NOT wired into the
readiness probe because a transient blip in any of these shouldn't
make the pod un-routable (cached / non-LLM endpoints still serve,
and Redis-backed work falls back to inline execution). Redis lives
here rather than in /ready precisely because it is a background-only
dependency: a Redis outage degrades digests / audio prerender /
pre-trip jobs but must not take the synchronous chat path offline.

Behaviour:

- Skipped checks (no API key configured) report ``skipped``;
  not a failure.
- Reachable checks report ``ok``.
- Unreachable checks report ``error: <reason>`` but the response
  still returns 200 — degraded signal lives in the body, not the
  HTTP status. Alerting consumes the body.

The Anthropic check uses a cheap ``count_tokens`` API call when
available (no token billing); falls back to a TCP-connect probe
when the SDK doesn't expose count_tokens.

IP-rate-limited (not token-gated — legitimate uptime monitors rarely
carry a service token). Each hit fires a real Anthropic call AND a
real Tavily search, and Tavily's circuit breaker counts probe
failures as real signal — an unthrottled loop here can burn Tavily
quota and trip the shared web-search breaker, failing real user
searches while every dashboard still reads green.

BlockAlternativesResponse.title: BlockAlternativesResponse
BlockAlternativesResponse.description:
``GET /api/trips/{trip_id}/itinerary/blocks/{block_id}/alternatives``.

The CandidateSet for a block's Swap surface. ``reason`` is set (and
``candidates`` empty) when there's nothing to offer — e.g. ``no_venue``
for a free-time / experience block.

BookingActionResponse.title: BookingActionResponse
BookingActionResponse.description: Shared response model for booking mutation endpoints.

BookingCancellationTruth.title: BookingCancellationTruth
BookingCancellationTruth.description: Viewer-safe, exact cancellation lifecycle derived from provider evidence.

BookingOfferViewerAccess.title: BookingOfferViewerAccess
BookingOfferViewerAccess.description: Server-authored viewer posture for provider-sensitive booking reads.

BridgeRenderRequest.title: BridgeRenderRequest
BridgeRenderRequest.description: Render a bridge sentence to audio for inline playback.

BridgeRenderResponse.title: BridgeRenderResponse
BridgeRenderResponse.description:
Inline base64 audio data URL for the bridge.

Phase 6b.3 v1 returns the audio inline so the client can hand
it directly to ``adapter.setSource()`` without an extra round-trip.
Phase 8 can switch to CDN URLs once R2 is wired and bridge audio
starts being cached server-side.

CancelTurnResponse.title: CancelTurnResponse
CancelTurnResponse.description: Result of an ownership-scoped explicit turn cancellation request.

CaptureMomentRequest.title: CaptureMomentRequest
CaptureMomentRequest.description:
Body for POST /api/trips/{tid}/observations.

The capture-nudge card surfaces a soft prompt at evening-during-trip;
the user types a short reflection and submits. The body is text-only
in Phase 4 — photo capture lands in Phase 5 once the media pipeline
(S3 / CDN / size limits) is in place.

``content`` is the captured text. ``importance`` is exposed so future
surfaces (voice agent transcribed memories, multi-line journal) can
bias persistence; the home capture-nudge passes the default 5.

Caveat.title: Caveat
Caveat.description:
Structured because caveats get special UI treatment on the phone —
they can't be buried in the body (allergen warnings, accessibility, etc.).

ChatHistoryMessage.title: ChatHistoryMessage
ChatHistoryMessage.description: A single message in the chat history.

device_id.title: Device Id
device_id.description: Stable per-device identifier from the client

CollectionDetail.title: CollectionDetail
CollectionDetail.description: Full collection payload: metadata + ordered hydrated members.

CollectionSummary.title: CollectionSummary
CollectionSummary.description: Guides-row card payload: just what the row needs to render.

ComposeBoardRequest.title: ComposeBoardRequest
ComposeBoardRequest.description: Body for ``POST /api/atlas/compose`` — compose from a full query object.

CompositionResult.title: CompositionResult
CompositionResult.description: The assembled board / list / empty-state for one taste query.

CompositionUnderstanding.title: CompositionUnderstanding
CompositionUnderstanding.description:
What the engine understood and actually used.

This powers the board's quiet trust affordance ("Built from…") and product
telemetry. It is intentionally plain: no raw private prose beyond the
already-returned residual phrase, and no invented reasoning.

ConciergeCardFeedbackRequest.title: ConciergeCardFeedbackRequest
ConciergeCardFeedbackRequest.description: Structured feedback for one Concierge Home card.

ConciergeCardLifecycleRequest.title: ConciergeCardLifecycleRequest
ConciergeCardLifecycleRequest.description: Lifecycle transition for a durable Vesper Home card.

ConfirmTripCreationProposalRequest.title: ConfirmTripCreationProposalRequest
ConfirmTripCreationProposalRequest.description: Version the user actually reviewed before pressing the primary action.

ConflictDismissalRequest.title: ConflictDismissalRequest
ConflictDismissalRequest.description:
Identify a conflict instance to dismiss/restore. ``block_ids`` are the
conflict's affected blocks (the FE echoes them from the DayConflict).

``conflict_type`` is validated against the known conflict enum so an
arbitrary free-text string cannot be stored as a dismissal key.
``block_ids`` is capped at 64 entries × 36-char UUIDs to bound writes from a
malformed or adversarial payload while still covering a realistically busy
day: day-scoped conflicts (``budget_overrun``, ``over_packed``,
``scattered_day``) emit one block id PER block in the day, so a 20+ block day
overflowed the old cap of 20 → 422 → the optimistic dismiss rolled back and
the conflict re-fired forever. 64 covers any plausible single day. The cap
only bounds the list length; the stored ``conflict_key`` (sorted+joined ids)
stays stable and still matches what the scanner produces, so existing
dismissals keep suppressing.

ConflictReceipt.title: ConflictReceipt
ConflictReceipt.description: ConflictRow — two overlapping blocks (Urgent · problem / Orient · clash).

ConversationAgencyResponse.title: ConversationAgencyResponse
ConversationAgencyResponse.description: How Vesper is allowed to participate in this group room.

ConversationListItem.title: ConversationListItem
ConversationListItem.description:
Lightweight conversation for list views.

Enriched fields are populated by ``get_conversation_list_for_user``
so the history surface can render last-message preview + unread
count without follow-up fetches per row. Older clients that don't
need the enrichment still receive the legacy shape — the new
fields default to None / 0 so a Pydantic decode against existing
snapshots stays happy.

planning_intent_bootstrap.description: Optional onboarding draft to seed intent_state on create. Used by the talk-it-through path so Vesper can promote without re-asking for fields the user already supplied.

CreateSideChatRequest.title: CreateSideChatRequest
CreateSideChatRequest.description: Create a personal side conversation linked to a trip.

CreateSideChatResponse.title: CreateSideChatResponse
CreateSideChatResponse.description: Response after creating a side chat.

destination.title: Destination
destination.description: Human-readable destination label captured when a client starts a trip from destination-led UI. Persisted into trip_summary.

CrossTripThreadPriorTripView.title: CrossTripThreadPriorTripView
CrossTripThreadPriorTripView.description: Per-prior-trip chip data hydrated at read time on the API edge.

CrossTripThreadView.title: CrossTripThreadView
CrossTripThreadView.description:
API response shape — hydrates prior_trip_ids into title chips
so the frontend doesn't need a second fetch per chip.

DataExportResponse.title: DataExportResponse
DataExportResponse.description:
A DSAR data-access bundle: every row we hold that belongs to the
user, grouped by table. Binary fields are summarized, not dumped.

DeckBriefDay.title: DeckBriefDay
DeckBriefDay.description: One row in a Brief's artifact preview — a drafted or open itinerary day.

DeckBriefSubstrate.title: DeckBriefSubstrate
DeckBriefSubstrate.description:
Brief substrate — review a thing Vesper drafted (a plan). The artifact IS
the substrate: a compact day skeleton from real itinerary data, open days
shown. Actions are review/refine, not a choice among options.

DeckCallSubstrate.title: DeckCallSubstrate
DeckCallSubstrate.description:
Call decision substrate — one yes/no with a deadline (held booking) or a
conflict to resolve. ``variant`` picks the face shape; the actions carry a
``kind`` the FE maps to a real handler (confirm/reject a booking, find an
alternative, keep). All display values are real card data, never invented.

DeckFocus.title: DeckFocus
DeckFocus.description:
Level-2 decision substrate — the "help me decide" content behind a card.

Distinct from ``DeckStructured`` (raw options/rows): focus carries Vesper's
JUDGMENT layer rendered full-screen in the Deck. The home glance presents the
decision; this helps make it.

``layout`` is the decision SHAPE — ``pick`` (choose one of N), ``call`` (a
yes/no with a deadline / a conflict), ``brief`` (review a drafted thing), or
``near_you`` (ambient what's-good-around-you). Each layout fills its own
sub-payload; the others stay None. Structure is real data; the Pick's
reasoning/tradeoff come from a grounded LLM pass.

ORPHAN (canon 146 §03b / D3): ``compare`` is FE-only today — not a layout
literal here. Wire a producer when D5 supplies a genuine two-option stay
decision; until then the FE face is dormant.

DeckNearYouPlace.title: DeckNearYouPlace
DeckNearYouPlace.description: One nearby venue row in the ambient Near-You deck face.

DeckNearYouSubstrate.title: DeckNearYouSubstrate
DeckNearYouSubstrate.description:
Ambient Near-You substrate — what's good around you right now.

Not a booking decision (that's Pick). Real nearby venues + walk time +
why-it-fits, with saved/loved tags. Suppress-when-thin: never emit without
≥1 named place.

DeckOption.title: DeckOption
DeckOption.description: One option in a Vote or Flight deck face.

DeckPerson.title: DeckPerson
DeckPerson.description: One person row — voter in Vote/Flight, counterparty in Settle.

DeckPickCandidate.title: DeckPickCandidate
DeckPickCandidate.description:
One candidate in a Pick decision — the chosen one or a rejected option.

The structure (``name``/``walk``/``price``/``attribute``) is DATA — pulled
straight from the ranked venues, never hallucinated. The judgment
(``why_not``, and the ``leading`` flag's reasoning) is generated grounded in
that structure. All display values are pre-formatted by the backend.

DeckReadinessRow.title: DeckReadinessRow
DeckReadinessRow.description:
One open-loop row in a departure-readiness deck face.

A resolvable pre-departure gap (stay not booked, open days, a decision
waiting). ``resolved`` rows render checked; open rows render highlighted.
Status-only — the deck's single primary action routes to where they're
closed (no per-row buttons, so nothing looks tappable that isn't).

DeckSettlementTransfer.title: DeckSettlementTransfer
DeckSettlementTransfer.description:
One exact net transfer in a Settle face.

Unlike the legacy ``expense_ids`` bridge, this is the same durable money
instruction returned by the settlement API.  The client can therefore
record (or remind about) the transfer without guessing which expense
shares produced the net balance.

DeckStructured.title: DeckStructured
DeckStructured.description:
Discriminated structured payload for rich Deck card faces.

Cards without this field render the generic prose face. Cards with it
switch on ``layout`` to render the appropriate face component. All
display values are pre-formatted by the backend (e.g. "€128") so the
FE never re-derives money or colour hex values.

Layout names describe the decision grammar, not merely the visual family.
``vote`` and ``readiness`` remain accepted as migration aliases for stored
cards, but new producers emit one of the explicit variants.

DeleteAccountResponse.title: DeleteAccountResponse
DeleteAccountResponse.description:
Summary of a completed account deletion — per-table row counts plus
how many shared resources were handed off to other members.

DerivedSignal.title: DerivedSignal
DerivedSignal.description:
One taste signal extracted from an artifact. Visible in the audit bridge.

See the V0 plan's Signal extraction section for the provenance contract:
every signal carries a back-reference to its source artifact, plus an
optional ``observation_id`` linking to the underlying observation row.

DigestRetryRequest.title: DigestRetryRequest
DigestRetryRequest.description:
Targeted retry for trips whose background digest failed.

``trip_ids`` is the list of UUIDs returned by
``run_digest_for_active_trips`` in its ``failed_trip_ids`` field —
typically surfaced in the warning log and passed directly here by ops.

``date`` overrides the target date; defaults to yesterday (the same
date the background loop would have used).  Pass an ISO date string
if you need to backfill a specific day.

DigestRunResponse.title: DigestRunResponse
DigestRunResponse.description: Result of a manual digest run.

DiscoverFeedProvenance.title: DiscoverFeedProvenance
DiscoverFeedProvenance.description: Display-safe authorship and trust context for a feed item.

DiscoverMapPin.title: DiscoverMapPin
DiscoverMapPin.description:
One placeable Discover entity. ``lat``/``lng`` are always finite (the
builder drops null-geometry rows). ``take`` / ``affinity`` are the
differentiating judgment layer — both optional (honest absence).

``saved`` / ``in_trip`` are the viewer's own EXPLICIT relationship with the
place, kept as their own channel: they never change the pin's verdict fill
(that would fabricate a Vesper stance), only its form/label. ``in_trip`` is
only ever true in the ``place`` scope — a live trip to this city — since the
``nearby`` scope has no destination to match a plan against.

DiscoverMapTakePreview.title: DiscoverMapTakePreview
DiscoverMapTakePreview.description:
The judgment-on-the-pin: a curator's verdict + one-line headline.

This is what makes the map a BROWSE surface rather than a lookup — the pin
carries Vesper's stance, not just a dot. Sourced from the shared
``entity_takes`` curator tier (no per-pin LLM call); the full Take is a
tap-time fetch on the peek card.

DiscoveryReflection.title: DiscoveryReflection
DiscoveryReflection.description:
Response for ``GET /api/atlas/discovery-reflection``.

The Discover→Atlas half of the flywheel: "places you saved before
{destination} that became part of the trip." Atlas renders the reflection
from ``items`` (the Discover-sourced saves tied to the destination) under
``headline``. ``destination_name`` echoes the resolved destination; ``items``
is empty (and ``headline`` null) when there is nothing real to reflect —
honest, never fabricated.

DiscoveryReflectionItem.title: DiscoveryReflectionItem
DiscoveryReflectionItem.description:
One place the traveler DISCOVERED + SAVED in Discover that became part of
a trip / destination.

Grounded in a real ``traveler_place_affinity`` row whose save carried a
Discover ``signal_source`` (``evidence[].source`` contains ``discover``) — so
every item is a genuine "I found this in Discover, before the trip" save,
never inferred. ``destination_name`` is the entity's enclosing city (the
reflection's grouping key); ``entity_category`` the kind-of-place.

DismissCardRequest.title: DismissCardRequest
DismissCardRequest.description:
Body for POST /api/trips/{tid}/home_cards/dismiss.

Identifies the card by its (archetype, ref_id) tuple — the same shape
the GET response surfaces. ``ref_id`` is TEXT to accommodate place
slugs alongside UUIDs.

DismissCardResponse.title: DismissCardResponse
DismissCardResponse.description:
Acknowledgment payload — the frontend uses ``status`` to drive the
undo toast. ``dismissed`` is the tuple just affected so the client
has everything it needs to call the undo endpoint without re-deriving
keys.

DissolveParallelPlanPayload.title: DissolveParallelPlanPayload
DissolveParallelPlanPayload.description: Server-authored reversal of one parallel-plan creation.

DnaConfidence.title: DnaConfidence
DnaConfidence.description: How sure the agent is about the DNA dimensions. Powers provisional copy.

DnaPhraseDisputeRequest.title: DnaPhraseDisputeRequest
DnaPhraseDisputeRequest.description:
Body for ``POST /api/me/dna-phrases/dispute``.

The user tapped "not quite" on a phrase rendered by the Me-tab
Travel DNA card. ``phrase`` is the exact rendered string so the
observation can carry the user-visible text into Personal Memory
synthesis verbatim.

DossierExemplarItem.title: DossierExemplarItem
DossierExemplarItem.description:
A concrete entity (venue/experience) referenced by a format-aware dossier.

Used to render inline save cards in the spot/experience reader.

EntityTake.title: EntityTake
EntityTake.description: A cached Take row — what the DB stores and the API returns.

ExpenseMutationCapability.title: ExpenseMutationCapability
ExpenseMutationCapability.description:
Server-authored mutation posture for the current expense viewer.

This is advisory UX truth, not the write authority: update/delete still
repeat the same authorization and live-payment checks transactionally.

ExpenseShareInput.title: ExpenseShareInput
ExpenseShareInput.description: Used in ExpenseCreate/Update to define shares inline.

FacetChip.title: FacetChip
FacetChip.description:
One visible, editable parse term. ``sensitive`` chips (companion/person)
force ``visibility: private`` and never serialize into a shareable board.

text.title: Text
text.description: The user's free-text feedback. 4000-char limit matches App Store What's-New.

screen.title: Screen
screen.description: Screen path the user was on when they tapped Send Feedback (e.g. 'trip-home', 'me/memory').

trip_id.title: Trip Id
trip_id.description: Trip the user was viewing, if any. Helps reproduce.

GeoPoint.title: GeoPoint
GeoPoint.description: Single map point — a photo cluster centroid or named place.

GeofenceEvent.title: GeofenceEvent
GeofenceEvent.description:
One geofence trigger the server observed.

Append-only: every row represents a moment in time, immutable after
insert. ``manually_narrated`` is the catch-all for cases where the
user tapped the entity card without ever entering its geofence — we
still record it so the observability dashboard reflects total
narration attempts, geofence-driven or not.

GlobalDossierFeedResponse.title: GlobalDossierFeedResponse
GlobalDossierFeedResponse.description: Random cross-city dossier sample for the global Discover feed.

GroupedResults.title: GroupedResults
GroupedResults.description: One typed section: a header label plus its ranked items.

HandoffNarrationContext.title: HandoffNarrationContext
HandoffNarrationContext.description:
The context block the voice agent reads before its first turn.

Includes what the user heard, what was about to play, and the
trigger transcript — enough to ground the agent's reply without
a separate context fetch.

HeadingBlock.title: HeadingBlock
HeadingBlock.description:
A section heading. ``level`` is 2 or 3 — we deliberately don't
expose h1 because the Take headline is the de facto h1 of the card.

active_in_chat.title: Active In Chat
active_in_chat.description: Is the chat screen foregrounded right now?

headphones_connected.title: Headphones Connected
headphones_connected.description: Any audio output that's not the phone speaker?

motion.title: Motion
motion.description: Coarse activity: still / walking / running / cycling / driving / unknown

ambient_db.title: Ambient Db
ambient_db.description: Optional ambient noise estimate in dB

trip_id.title: Trip Id
trip_id.description: Trip the user is currently engaged with, if any.

device_id.title: Device Id
device_id.description: Stable per-device identifier; required when trip_id is set.

HeartbeatRequest.title: HeartbeatRequest
HeartbeatRequest.description:
Compact physical-context signal from the client.

All fields optional — clients may have permission for some signals
but not others (e.g., motion without microphone for ambient_db).

HeroIdentity.title: HeroIdentity
HeroIdentity.description: The one-line identity: place + the single most relevant status fact.

HeroPill.title: HeroPill
HeroPill.description:
Top-of-card status pill: a marker (countdown / liveness) + register tone.

Far-out planning + Tempt states carry this; it drives the FE's rich
FarShell layout. ``tone``: ink = planning/dates · sage = dreaming ·
gold = Vesper pull · amber = needs-you.

HeroRead.title: HeroRead
HeroRead.description: The one read in Vesper's voice. ``prose`` is the only LLM-authored field.

HeroStep.title: HeroStep
HeroStep.description: The one step — a door, never a transaction.

temporal_anchor.title: Temporal Anchor
temporal_anchor.description: Compact temporal cue — 'Day 3 of 5' / 'in 11 days' / '5 days ago'.

phase.title: Phase
phase.description: Calendar-relative phase for home copy and ambient framing: ideation | pre | during | post — from ``compute_phase_info(trip)`` (today vs trip start/end). Intentionally different from ``TripMapState.phase`` on the map read model, which is trip-status-relative (ideation | planning | final_prep | active | completed) and answers lifecycle/booking readiness rather than “where are we on the calendar?”.

HomeAnchor.title: HomeAnchor
HomeAnchor.description: Single always-present orientation card at the top of the feed.

archetype.title: Archetype
archetype.description: Discriminator for render + tap routing. Active zone: 'pending_decision' | 'morning_brief' | 'booking_event' | 'constraint_meal' | 'capture_nudge' | 'just_accepted' | 'pending_too_long' | 'unread_intake' | 'planning_brief' | 'trip_retrospective' | 'settlement_closeout'. Ambient zone: 'happening_nearby' | 'place_context' | 'memory_highlight' | 'next_trip_inspo'.

ref_id.title: Ref Id
ref_id.description: UUID-as-string for the underlying entity (proposal / experience / digest), or place slug for place_context.

day_number.title: Day Number
day_number.description: 1-based itinerary day index when the card naturally targets one day (e.g. morning digest). Omit when trip-wide or not applicable.

tier.title: Tier
tier.description: Phase 3 urgency tier driving in-zone ranking and dismissal rules. 1 = urgent / blocking (non-dismissable), 2 = this trip, 3 = informational / ambient. Phase 3 sets tier statically per archetype; deadline-driven dynamic promotion arrives in Phase 5.

HomeCard.title: HomeCard
HomeCard.description:
One follow-on card. Archetype discriminates render + tap target.

Phase 2 archetypes:
- ``pending_decision`` — open proposal awaiting group input (active)
- ``morning_brief`` — today's digest restated as a card (active)
- ``happening_nearby`` — taste-DNA-ranked experience for the destination (ambient)
- ``place_context`` — curated destination angle worth knowing (ambient)

active.title: Active
active.description: Cards needing user attention — decisions, briefings.

ambient.title: Ambient
ambient.description: Quieter discovery/context cards — happenings, place angles.

HomeFeed.title: HomeFeed
HomeFeed.description:
Full payload for ``GET /trips/{trip_id}/home_cards``.

Three zones; clients render top-to-bottom: anchor, active, ambient.

HoodReceipt.title: HoodReceipt
HoodReceipt.description: Hood — one coarse neighbourhood character read (Match).

HydratedMember.title: HydratedMember
HydratedMember.description:
One member with its entity type tag + lightweight entity payload.

The entity payload is shaped by the DB layer; the shape varies per
entity type (a venue has `name`, a dossier has `angle`, etc.). The
mobile app switches on `entity_type` to render.

Hypothesis.title: Hypothesis
Hypothesis.description:
Internal-only. The LLM's working claim about the user and its evidence.

NOT surfaced to the user. Used for:
- Debugging + eval ("why did the Take emphasize X?")
- Offline analysis (aggregating which hypothesis categories correlate
  with saves vs dismisses, feeding Personal Memory refresh)
- Grounding validation (post-generation check that every hypothesis
  references real evidence)

`evidence_refs` are opaque ID strings scoped by `evidence_type`:
- personal_memory: the Personal Memory version id or a section marker
- past_trip: trip_id
- angle_affinity: angle_id
- group_signal: group_profile_version id
- saved_entity: entity_id previously saved

InitialPlanCommitmentState.title: InitialPlanCommitmentState
InitialPlanCommitmentState.description:
Intrinsic softness that may be asserted when a first plan is born.

Provider and traveler-confirmed states require their own canonical
chronology.  Keeping them out of ``materialize_shape`` prevents an
accepted draft from manufacturing booking or occurrence truth.

IntakePayload.title: IntakePayload
IntakePayload.description:
Bulk chip payload for minimal onboarding.

Each field is optional. Sections use replace-within-section semantics:
submitting ``dietary=["vegan"]`` deactivates any other active dietary rows
for the user. Omitting a field leaves that section untouched, so callers
can update one section without disturbing others. An empty list clears
the section.

entity_id.title: Entity Id
entity_id.description: The narrated entity's id (str or uuid)

playback_position_ms.title: Playback Position Ms
playback_position_ms.description: Position into the audio when the event fired. Null for dismissed/interrupted.

trip_phase.title: Trip Phase
trip_phase.description: pre_trip | during_trip | post_trip — set by client when known

audio_position_ms.title: Audio Position Ms
audio_position_ms.description: Where in the audio the user was when they interrupted.

trigger_transcript.title: Trigger Transcript
trigger_transcript.description: The user's speech that triggered the interrupt — handed to the voice agent so it can respond directly to what was asked.

InterruptedNarrationContext.title: InterruptedNarrationContext
InterruptedNarrationContext.description: What the client knows about the narration that was interrupted.

InviteAcceptRequest.title: InviteAcceptRequest
InviteAcceptRequest.description:
Optional accept-time body. Absent/None claimed_intake is the
ordinary accept path (single-use invites, or a group-share invitee
who never answered / declined to claim).

InviteClaimIntake.title: InviteClaimIntake
InviteClaimIntake.description:
What the client asserts it remembers submitting pre-auth, offered
at accept time for a group-share invite (D10, attribute-at-accept —
invitee-postjoin design, Slice H). Same shape as InviteIntakeRequest's
signal fields; the backend matches this against the invite's
anonymous ``pending_intake.submissions`` list rather than trusting it
outright — see claim_group_share_intake.

InviteConversationSummary.title: InviteConversationSummary
InviteConversationSummary.description:
Landing-page facts for a conversation-scoped invite (pre-trip).

Lighter than :class:`InviteTripSummary` — the conversation is still
exploratory, so we render what the intent scratchpad has captured
so far rather than committed trip details.

InviteIntakeRequest.title: InviteIntakeRequest
InviteIntakeRequest.description:
The invitee's current pre-auth draft.

New clients send a stable anonymous ``client_submission_id`` plus a
monotonically increasing revision. That turns rapid chip toggles into
replace/clear semantics and lets the server ignore a late response from an
older tap. The fields remain optional for backward compatibility.

InviteListItem.title: InviteListItem
InviteListItem.description:
Serializable summary of one invite row, returned by list endpoints.

Used by the organizer's "Pending invites" UI — each item carries
enough state to render a status pill (sent / answered / joined /
revoked / expired) and the small momentum signal of a chip answer
when the recipient has already engaged pre-auth.

InviteSnapshot.title: InviteSnapshot
InviteSnapshot.description: Haiku-generated landing-page content for one invite view.

InviteTripSummary.title: InviteTripSummary
InviteTripSummary.description: Non-generated facts shown on the landing page alongside the snapshot.

ItineraryBlockOut.title: ItineraryBlockOut
ItineraryBlockOut.description: API response shape for a single itinerary block.

ItineraryBookingProjection.title: ItineraryBookingProjection
ItineraryBookingProjection.description: Viewer-safe Bookings/provider-detail projection over durable truth.

ItineraryHeldPriceDeclineRequest.title: ItineraryHeldPriceDeclineRequest
ItineraryHeldPriceDeclineRequest.description: Explicit controller decision to reject one current provider quote.

ItineraryHeldPriceReapprovalRequest.title: ItineraryHeldPriceReapprovalRequest
ItineraryHeldPriceReapprovalRequest.description: Fresh human approval bound to one persisted provider quote and saga revision.

ItineraryOperationCommitResult.title: ItineraryOperationCommitResult
ItineraryOperationCommitResult.description: Terminal IR-07 result; rejected attempts remain idempotent evidence.

ItineraryOperationConfirmation.title: ItineraryOperationConfirmation
ItineraryOperationConfirmation.description: User evidence bound to the exact preview being committed.

ItineraryOperationPreviewRequest.title: ItineraryOperationPreviewRequest
ItineraryOperationPreviewRequest.description:
One typed draft plus a caller-owned correlation identity.

``client_request_id`` is intentionally not an idempotency key and is not
included in semantic preview hashing. Commit idempotency begins in IR-07.

ItineraryOperationProposal.title: ItineraryOperationProposal
ItineraryOperationProposal.description: Canonical IR-08 proposal truth joined to the legacy read-model id.

ItineraryOperationProposalCreateRequest.title: ItineraryOperationProposalCreateRequest
ItineraryOperationProposalCreateRequest.description: Create a durable proposal from one exact normalized operation.

ItineraryPlanEffectSummary.title: ItineraryPlanEffectSummary
ItineraryPlanEffectSummary.description: Group-safe structural effect; never includes private rationale.

ItineraryProposalReadProjection.title: ItineraryProposalReadProjection
ItineraryProposalReadProjection.description: Compatibility fields consumed by the group-facing proposal projection.

ItineraryProviderBookingTruthProjection.title: ItineraryProviderBookingTruthProjection
ItineraryProviderBookingTruthProjection.description: One stable provider object linked to, but distinct from, its event.

ItineraryProviderSagaEventProjection.title: ItineraryProviderSagaEventProjection
ItineraryProviderSagaEventProjection.description: Group-safe provider history evidence; callback credentials stay private.

ItineraryReplanChange.title: ItineraryReplanChange
ItineraryReplanChange.description: One traveler-readable structural consequence of an atomic Replan.

ItineraryReplanTravel.title: ItineraryReplanTravel
ItineraryReplanTravel.description: Group-safe route consequence without geometry or planner rationale.

ItineraryWritebackCorrectionRequest.title: ItineraryWritebackCorrectionRequest
ItineraryWritebackCorrectionRequest.description: A replacement observation; the superseded identity comes from the route.

KeptBoard.title: KeptBoard
KeptBoard.description:
One saved board in the traveler's library.

The saved row is just the *query* — ``facet_set`` re-composes the board live
via the board endpoint, so a kept board is never frozen. ``title`` is the
cached display title for the list; ``created_at`` orders the library.

KeptBoardsResponse.title: KeptBoardsResponse
KeptBoardsResponse.description: The traveler's non-dismissed kept boards, newest first.

LatencyDistribution.title: LatencyDistribution
LatencyDistribution.description:
Percentile + spread summary for one latency series.

All values are integer milliseconds — the underlying column is an
INTEGER and we explicitly cast back so a float-formatted ``stddev``
doesn't leak into the response (would surprise frontend code that
keys off integer ms throughout).

LeaseInfo.title: LeaseInfo
LeaseInfo.description: Common shape for claim / get_active responses.

LeaveByActionResponse.title: LeaveByActionResponse
LeaveByActionResponse.description: Confirmation envelope for leave-by dismiss / mute actions.

LeaveByHint.title: LeaveByHint
LeaveByHint.description:
In-app leave-by hint for a trip's imminent next stop.

The READ twin of the push payload — same fields the push body encodes, but
structured for the client to render ("Leave by 14:45 · 12 min walk"). See
``get_leave_by_hint`` for how it's computed.

LeaveByHintResponse.title: LeaveByHintResponse
LeaveByHintResponse.description: The current leave-by hint for a trip, or null when there's nothing to show.

ListBlock.title: ListBlock
ListBlock.description: An ordered or bulleted list. Each item supports inline markdown.

LocationReading.title: LocationReading
LocationReading.description:
A single GPS reading from a client device.

Written to ``conversations.metadata_.last_location`` and read by
the concierge's origin resolver (15-min TTL). Piggybacks on message
send requests so the concierge always sees the freshest available
location at turn time without a separate round-trip.

ManifestEntry.title: ManifestEntry
ManifestEntry.description:
One entry in the offline-narration manifest.

Carries everything the client needs to render the entry (entity
metadata, depth tier) plus the audio URL to download. The
``audio_url`` is a relative path against the API base — the client
constructs the absolute URL itself so this works across dev /
staging / prod without server-side host detection.

MapMemberStay.title: MapMemberStay
MapMemberStay.description:
A per-person stay pin (split-lodging) for the trip map.

Distinct from ``MapBase`` (the trip's single shared base, derived from
the itinerary). These come from the ``trip_accommodations`` table — one
per member who recorded their own stay. Only stays with coordinates
(free-form lat/lng) are projected; typed-entity stays without resolved
coords are skipped. The route passes a viewer-filtered list, so a
member's ``private`` stay never reaches anyone but its owner.

geometry.title: Geometry
geometry.description: GeoJSON Polygon or MultiPolygon as a structured object.

MapNeighborhood.title: MapNeighborhood
MapNeighborhood.description:
A neighborhood/district polygon for the destination city.

Sourced from ``places`` rows whose ``place_type`` is one of
neighborhood | district | quarter | borough and whose ``parent_id``
matches the city-level place. Only rows with non-NULL PostGIS geometry
are included.

``geometry`` is a structured GeoJSON Polygon / MultiPolygon dict
(the ``ST_AsGeoJSON`` output parsed server-side). Earlier versions
of this contract shipped the raw JSON string under ``geojson``,
which forced clients to ``JSON.parse`` inside an already-JSON
response — wasteful for large city polygon sets.

MapPhoto.title: MapPhoto
MapPhoto.description:
A geotagged trip photo for the map photo layer (P3).

Sourced from ``trip_photos`` rows with non-null ``gps_lat`` / ``gps_lng``.
The ``cdn_url`` is extracted from the ``photo_record`` JSONB column.
Only group-visible (``visibility ∈ {group, group_and_learn}``) non-deleted
photos are included — the uploader's own ``private`` photos are excluded
from the shared map layer.

MapSavedPlace.title: MapSavedPlace
MapSavedPlace.description:
A user-saved venue near the trip destination (Layer 2 context pin).

Sourced from ``entity_saves`` joined to ``venues`` via a PostGIS proximity
query in ``backend/core/db/saves.py::list_saved_near_place``. Only venue
saves within ``radius_km`` of the destination centroid are projected; saves
for entities without resolvable geometry are skipped. Used by the map canvas
to render a soft "context" pin layer distinct from itinerary stops.

MapShareResponse.title: MapShareResponse
MapShareResponse.description:
Response for the interactive trip map share endpoint (P4).

The ``url`` is a public Mapbox GL JS preview page protected by an HMAC
token; the token is the only gate, no auth on the preview page itself.

status.title: Status
status.description: Commitment fold exposed on the map (booked | tentative | confirmed | cancelled). Plan / itinerary blocks carry richer lifecycle strings (suggested, proposed, handed_off, booking_in_progress, …); the map projection collapses those via ``_map_stop_public_status`` in ``backend/core/map_state/builder.py`` (lines 56–68).

route_geometry.title: Route Geometry
route_geometry.description: GeoJSON-derived walking tour path for experience blocks with a LINESTRING route_geometry stored in PostGIS. Each MapPoint is one vertex of the path. NULL for non-experience stops or experiences without route geometry.

MarkReadRequest.title: MarkReadRequest
MarkReadRequest.description: Advance read state and/or persist the reader's current viewport.

MaterializeShapeBlock.title: MaterializeShapeBlock
MaterializeShapeBlock.description: One stable block in the accepted first-draft shape snapshot.

MaterializeShapeDay.title: MaterializeShapeDay
MaterializeShapeDay.description: One dated day in the accepted first-draft shape snapshot.

MemoryHomeTripCard.title: MemoryHomeTripCard
MemoryHomeTripCard.description: Recent-trip card on the Memory home surface.

MemoryReceipt.title: MemoryReceipt
MemoryReceipt.description:
Memory — a loved past trip the read cites, shown with a place
illustration thumbnail (Recall). ``scene`` is a destination/illustration
key the FE maps to a riso variant.

MemoryResetResponse.title: MemoryResetResponse
MemoryResetResponse.description: Counts of rows deleted per source table — useful for confirmation UI.

message_id.title: Message Id
message_id.description: The flagged message's id (server or client-generated).

text.title: Text
text.description: The flagged message's content, for triage context.

trip_id.title: Trip Id
trip_id.description: Trip the thread belongs to, if any. Helps reproduce.

reason.title: Reason
reason.description: Optional free-text reason the user gave for flagging it.

Moment.title: Moment
Moment.description:
One ranked item in the result. ``place`` + ``context_line`` are grounded
in real affinity fields — never invented.

MoveCheckResult.title: MoveCheckResult
MoveCheckResult.description:
Response for GET /api/trips/{trip_id}/itinerary/blocks/{block_id}/move-check.

``overlaps`` are hard conflicts; travel-time booleans are soft warnings.
``would_create`` (X-CRIT-2) is the Phase 3 standing-watcher scan run
against the TARGET DAY's hypothetical post-move state — so the user sees
tight_hop / over_packed / dead_gap / budget_overrun warnings BEFORE
committing the move, not after.

MoveConflict.title: MoveConflict
MoveConflict.description: A block on the target day that overlaps the proposed time window.

MuteTodayRequest.title: MuteTodayRequest
MuteTodayRequest.description:
Optional body for the mute-today endpoint.

``local_date`` is the calendar date in the user's device timezone (ISO-8601,
e.g. "2026-05-26"). When provided, the mute row is keyed on this date rather
than today in UTC, preventing the UTC-vs-local skew that caused users in
far-ahead timezones (UTC+9+) to mute the wrong day.

NarrateCitation.title: NarrateCitation
NarrateCitation.description:
One traceable claim in the narration reply (M-2).

Frontend renders the ``claim`` substring as a clickable inline
marker in the rendered reply text; tapping reveals the source
fragment by ``fragment_index`` (1-based, matching the [N] indices
Sonnet saw in its prompt).

persona_override.title: Persona Override
persona_override.description: Optional override for the persona auto-resolved from the trip's destination. Eval/admin only — production clients should leave this null.

location.description: Optional GPS reading from the client at trigger time.

session_arc.title: Session Arc
session_arc.description: Legacy ephemeral arc text. Kept for backward compatibility; new clients should pass ``session_id`` instead and let the server load the arc from ``voice_guide_sessions``. When both are set, ``session_id`` wins — the persistent arc is the source of truth.

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: Optional voice-guide session this narration belongs to. When set and the session is open, the server uses the session's ``arc_summary`` as the prompt context (overriding any request-supplied ``session_arc``) and appends a one-line entry after a successful narration so the next call sees this stop in the arc. Best-effort throughout — a missing or closed session falls back to the legacy ``session_arc`` behavior without surfacing an error.

NarrateRequest.title: NarrateRequest
NarrateRequest.description: A geofence-triggered or 'I'm at X' arrival event.

NarrationEntity.title: NarrationEntity
NarrationEntity.description:
The place being narrated.

``id`` is a string at the wire level so UUID-keyed entities (experiences)
and integer-keyed entities (venues, sites, places) can share the
schema. Server-side coercion to int happens before retrieval — see
``_coerce_entity_id`` below.

NarrationHistoryItem.title: NarrationHistoryItem
NarrationHistoryItem.description: One distinct narration the user has heard (Atlas replay list).

NarrationManifest.title: NarrationManifest
NarrationManifest.description:
Manifest of all pre-rendered narration audio for a trip.

Phase Voice-1 / Phase 2.5. The client fetches this on a WiFi
connection pre-departure to download every cached narration to
local storage. Once downloaded, ``useNarrationAudio`` plays from
the local path when offline.

NarrationStop.title: NarrationStop
NarrationStop.description: One navigable stop in the trip's itinerary, with geofence metadata.

NearbyItem.title: NearbyItem
NearbyItem.description: A venue or site near a given point.

NearbyResponse.title: NearbyResponse
NearbyResponse.description: Nearby venues/sites around a geographic point.

NotificationStatsResponse.title: NotificationStatsResponse
NotificationStatsResponse.description: Aggregated engagement metrics for a trip's notification outcomes.

OnboardingSignalsCreate.title: OnboardingSignalsCreate
OnboardingSignalsCreate.description:
Body for ``POST /api/users/{user_id}/onboarding-signals``.

Non-constraint taste/pace signals from the onboarding flow.

``branch`` identifies which onboarding path produced these signals.
``interest_axis`` and ``pace_axis`` are soft preference labels — never
treated as hard constraints. ``source`` identifies the onboarding version
so signals can be versioned as the onboarding flow evolves.

OperationBlockContent.title: OperationBlockContent
OperationBlockContent.description: Complete planner-owned content carried by Add/Replace operations.

OperationRecoveryCapability.title: OperationRecoveryCapability
OperationRecoveryCapability.description: Current recovery truth, recomputed from live facts on every read.

OptimizeRouteSuggestion.title: OptimizeRouteSuggestion
OptimizeRouteSuggestion.description:
One block's new position in the optimized day order.

Returned beside one canonical compound-operation preview.

ParagraphBlock.title: ParagraphBlock
ParagraphBlock.description: A prose paragraph. Supports inline markdown in ``text``.

ParseQueryRequest.title: ParseQueryRequest
ParseQueryRequest.description: Body for ``POST /api/atlas/parse`` — a freeform natural-language taste query.

ParseQueryResponse.title: ParseQueryResponse
ParseQueryResponse.description:
The structured reading of a natural-language taste query.

``facet_set`` are the recognized (dimension, value) facets. Only supported
taste dimensions steer retrieval today; bound-but-unsupported facets are
surfaced and then reported as unused by the composition understanding.
``residual_phrase`` holds everything the parser could NOT bind at all
(companion names, budget, free text), kept verbatim rather than forced into
a fabricated dimension — the honesty discipline.

ParsedFacet.title: ParsedFacet
ParsedFacet.description:
One facet the parser recognized in the query.

Core taste facets map to the live retrieval vocabulary (cuisine / category /
neighborhood / experience_type / genre). A few common residual phrases may
also be bound to explicit *unsupported* dimensions (weather, time_of_day,
travel_mode, occasion, mood) so the UI can show what Vesper understood
without pretending retrieval can use it yet. ``confidence`` is the parser's
certainty for this one mapping.

PatchTripRequest.title: PatchTripRequest
PatchTripRequest.description:
User-editable trip fields.

Excludes internal concierge/background fields from direct client mutation.

PayoffBlockAside.title: PayoffBlockAside
PayoffBlockAside.description:
One itinerary-row inflection line for the invitee post-join payoff.

``block_id`` must reference a real block from the trip's current
itinerary — the generator validates this post-hoc against the actual
block-id set it was given (invitee-postjoin design, Slice H).

PendingInviteAnswer.title: PendingInviteAnswer
PendingInviteAnswer.description:
An invite the organizer hasn't acknowledged yet, where the recipient
left a pre-auth chip answer.

Surfaces neutral "Sam answered privately" momentum in the bell. Cleared when the
organizer opens ``GET /api/trips/{trip_id}/invites`` (the Pending
invites surface) — that endpoint stamps ``intake_seen_at = now()``
on every invite for the trip.

PendingVote.title: PendingVote
PendingVote.description: A proposal the user hasn't voted on.

PersonaBreakdown.title: PersonaBreakdown
PersonaBreakdown.description:
Per-persona TTFT slice. ``persona_id`` is null for turns that
didn't carry a persona_id (legacy rows; new turns always set it).

PersonalLeadFrame.title: PersonalLeadFrame
PersonalLeadFrame.description:
"Because you loved X" — a cross-city taste recall that personalises the
lead read. Fires only when the reader loved a *kind of place* (>=2 times,
in OTHER cities) that the city being read *provably offers* — a pattern, not
an anecdote; cross-city, so the recognition is earned, never confabulated.

``because`` is the Vesper-voiced recall phrase ("the natural-wine rooms you
kept finding in Lisbon"); ``category`` is the raw venue_type/site_type (for
the FE's lens styling + telemetry); ``recalled_from`` is the prior city.
Null on the lead = no earned pattern → render the plain quality lead.

PlaceDossierItem.title: PlaceDossierItem
PlaceDossierItem.description:
A place dossier listing item, carrying its source place so a city-level
feed can show which neighborhood each dossier came from.

PlaceMapNeighborhood.title: PlaceMapNeighborhood
PlaceMapNeighborhood.description:
A child neighborhood that has editorial content, as a drill-in map pin.

Only neighborhoods that (a) contributed at least one dossier to this feed
and (b) carry a centroid are surfaced — a pin therefore always corresponds
to browsable content, never a bare administrative boundary.

PlanBlockFlag.title: PlanBlockFlag
PlanBlockFlag.description: UI hints derived server-side for blocks.

PlanBookingSummary.title: PlanBookingSummary
PlanBookingSummary.description: Lightweight booking session row for the plan-state shell.

PlanCommitmentState.title: PlanCommitmentState
PlanCommitmentState.description: Server-derived commitment lane for an itinerary block.

id.title: Id
id.description: Opaque decision identifier; for ``change_proposal`` equals the change-proposal UUID (see ``_proposal_to_plan_decision``).

PlanNextItem.title: PlanNextItem
PlanNextItem.description: Next actionable moment on the timeline (best-effort).

PlanReasoning.title: PlanReasoning
PlanReasoning.description:
Trip-level planner reasoning — the "How Vesper planned this" disclosure.

Each field is one stage of the planner's thinking; only non-empty stages are
populated (the builder drops blanks). Version-immutable. None when the
itinerary wasn't agent-generated (e.g. a manual/older version).

source_attribution.title: Source Attribution
source_attribution.default: Based on a shared Vesper story.

PlanSimilarSeed.title: PlanSimilarSeed
PlanSimilarSeed.description:
Public "plan something like this" seed derived from a shared story.

Privacy-safe by construction: built only from the ``PublicTripStory``
projection, so it carries no private inputs. A viewer can fetch this
without logging in (to see what they'd be starting) — account creation
is only required to actually create the derived trip.

PlanTravelSegment.title: PlanTravelSegment
PlanTravelSegment.description: Mirrors ``BlockTransition`` — extras allowed for booking-specific keys.

PlanningIntentBootstrap.title: PlanningIntentBootstrap
PlanningIntentBootstrap.description: Onboarding trip draft fields mirrored from the mobile client.

PlanningSavedPlaceBootstrap.title: PlanningSavedPlaceBootstrap
PlanningSavedPlaceBootstrap.description: A saved place the client used as a trip-draft starting point.

PostJoinPayoff.title: PostJoinPayoff
PostJoinPayoff.description:
The invitee post-join "felt, not told" acknowledgment moment.
