None (not an instance of this model) signals "nothing plausibly
matched" — the caller degrades to the plain TripBody mode, per the
invitee-postjoin design's own rule that absence must never render as
an apology or placeholder.

PostJoinPayoffResponse.title: PostJoinPayoffResponse
PostJoinPayoffResponse.description:
Response for the one-shot invitee payoff fetch (Slice H4).

``payoff=None`` covers every "show the ordinary trip" case alike —
already seen, no signal was ever given, or nothing plausibly
matched — the client never needs to distinguish them.

PostcardRenderResponse.title: PostcardRenderResponse
PostcardRenderResponse.description:
Outcome of a postcard render request.

``render_status`` is ``ready`` (rendered_image_url set), ``failed`` (a stable
``error`` token explains why — e.g. ``feature_disabled``, ``daily_cap_reached``,
``no_source_image``, ``below_quality_floor``), or ``none``.

PrerenderHintResponse.title: PrerenderHintResponse
PrerenderHintResponse.description: Response shape for the speculative pre-render endpoint.

ProactiveEvent.title: ProactiveEvent
ProactiveEvent.description: One append-only row describing autonomous agent work.

ProactiveNotification.title: ProactiveNotification
ProactiveNotification.description:
A proactive in-app notification from the notification intelligence system.

Sourced from ``notification_deliveries`` (channel='in_app') joined with
``notification_outcomes`` so the feed can surface personalised nudges
alongside conversation unread counts and proposal votes.

ProfileFactDisputeRequest.title: ProfileFactDisputeRequest
ProfileFactDisputeRequest.description:
Body for ``POST /api/me/profile-fact/dispute``.

Records a "not quite" reaction on a non-phrase profile fact — a
taste-dimension reading or an interest noun. Companion to
``dispute_dna_phrase``; lives in the "What I know about you"
transparency surface that disputes multiple facets from one screen.

``kind`` discriminates the observation copy. ``value`` is the
user-visible string the user just disputed (e.g. ``"Planner"`` or
``"seafood"``). ``label`` is optional context — for taste dims it
carries the dimension name (``"energy"`` / ``"planning"`` / …) so
the observation reads naturally; ignored for interests.

force.title: Force
force.description: Override the readiness gate. By default we require at least one destination and a date window in the intent scratchpad; set force=True to promote anyway (e.g. for testing).

PromoteToTripRequest.title: PromoteToTripRequest
PromoteToTripRequest.description: Explicit promotion of a pre-trip conversation into a committed trip.

ProposalDetail.title: ProposalDetail
ProposalDetail.description:
Full proposal detail.

When ``proposed_entity_type`` + ``proposed_entity_id`` are set, the
mobile app deep-links the proposal card into that entity's detail
view, where a per-viewer personalized Take is fetched via
``POST /api/takes``. Both are null for proposals that don't point
at a single entity (e.g. pure reschedule, "add a day").

``voting_enabled`` mirrors the trip-level flag — the mobile app uses
it to decide whether to render the vote widget (enabled) or a
reaction-only view with an "ask organizer to resolve" affordance
(disabled).

ProposalImpactAnalysis.title: ProposalImpactAnalysis
ProposalImpactAnalysis.description:
Structured trade-off payload for a change proposal.

All fields are optional so the concierge can fill in as much or as
little as warranted by the proposal stakes. ``extra="allow"`` preserves
legacy keys (e.g. ``new_time``, ``new_title``, ``new_notes``) written
by older proposals and consumed by ``proposal_apply._apply_*``.

ProposalSummary.title: ProposalSummary
ProposalSummary.description:
Lightweight proposal for list views.

Deliberately omits ``proposed_entity_*`` — the deep-link affordance
is a detail-page concern, not a list-row concern.

``my_vote`` is the authenticated viewer's current vote ("approve",
"reject", "neutral") or ``None`` if they haven't voted yet. The
mobile client uses this to filter the "Needs your vote" digest.

ProviderBreakdown.title: ProviderBreakdown
ProviderBreakdown.description:
Per-STT-provider TTFT slice (V-3). Surfaces the latency cost of
the on-device fallback (Apple SpeechAnalyzer / Picovoice) vs. the
cloud baseline (Deepgram). Once the iOS native module ships and
starts emitting ``stt_provider != 'deepgram_cloud'``, this is the
surface that lights up to compare them.

PublicProfile.title: PublicProfile
PublicProfile.description:
Public-safe view of another traveler — what a viewer sees before they
follow. DEFAULT-DENY: taste_phrases are the display-safe concierge phrases
(never hard constraints / budget / health), and stories are only the
owner's follower/link-visible ones.

PublicProfileStory.title: PublicProfileStory
PublicProfileStory.description: A public-safe story summary on a profile — links to the public page.

PublicReason.title: PublicReason
PublicReason.description: A reason that is safe to show in any channel.

PublicStorySection.title: PublicStorySection
PublicStorySection.description: A public-safe narrative section — body only, no internal ids.

PublicStoryViewerContext.title: PublicStoryViewerContext
PublicStoryViewerContext.description:
Authenticated open-in-app routing hint for a public story slug.

This is deliberately separate from ``PublicTripStory`` so anonymous public
projection payloads never expose owner identity or trip ids.

privacy_note.title: Privacy Note
privacy_note.default: A real trip memory, shared intentionally - private details removed.

PublicTripStory.title: PublicTripStory
PublicTripStory.description:
The PUBLIC projection of a Trip Story.

DEFAULT-DENY ALLOWLIST. This model is the *complete* set of fields a
non-owner may ever see. It deliberately omits ``user_id``, ``trip_id``,
``version``, section ids, ``anchor_observation_id``, ``edited_at``,
``composer_model``, and every private input (constraints, budget,
health, fatigue, observations). Build it ONLY via
``backend.core.story_projection.build_public_story_payload`` so the
allowlist is enforced in exactly one place.

ReactRequest.title: ReactRequest
ReactRequest.description: React to a structured message (reaction_card or vote_widget).

ocr_quality.title: Ocr Quality
ocr_quality.description: Classify review readiness without inventing confidence scores.

ReceiptOcrResult.title: ReceiptOcrResult
ReceiptOcrResult.description: Legible facts extracted from a receipt, never inferred defaults.

RecommendationAction.title: RecommendationAction
RecommendationAction.description: A reviewed, typed CTA derived alongside recommendation reasoning.

move.title: Move
move.description: One-line verdict — 'the move is X because Y'. Opinionated.

why_for_group.title: Why For Group
why_for_group.description: Why this fits THIS group (energy, constraints, dynamic).

why_for_person.title: Why For Person
why_for_person.description: Why this fits the requester specifically (personal memory, prior preferences).

what_to_notice.title: What To Notice
what_to_notice.description: Connoisseur notes — what to look for, order, ask about.

what_to_skip.title: What To Skip
what_to_skip.description: What to avoid or deprioritize at this place.

timing_note.title: Timing Note
timing_note.description: When to go, how long, reservation tip, time-of-day cue.

actions.title: Actions
actions.description: Typed phone or calendar actions; never infer actions from display prose.

RecommendationPayload.title: RecommendationPayload
RecommendationPayload.description: Structured reasoning attached to a concierge recommendation.

accuracy_m.title: Accuracy M
accuracy_m.description: Reported GPS accuracy in meters; null when client can't supply it.

RecordGeofenceEventRequest.title: RecordGeofenceEventRequest
RecordGeofenceEventRequest.description:
Body for ``POST /api/trips/{trip_id}/geofence-events``.

The ``user_id`` is taken from auth — clients can only record their
own events. Attempting to specify someone else would be a privacy
leak and is rejected at the application layer regardless.

RecordGeofenceEventResponse.title: RecordGeofenceEventResponse
RecordGeofenceEventResponse.description:
Composite response: the event row + the session it's bound to.

When ``event_type='entered'`` and no session is active, the route
opens one and returns it inline so the client doesn't need a second
round-trip to learn its session_id. When the caller enters a different
entity, the prior session is closed with ``close_reason='next_place'``
by ``open_session`` and a fresh arc opens. Otherwise ``session`` is
null (the trigger was recorded but no session changes resulted) or
the existing active session.

RedactionPolicy.title: RedactionPolicy
RedactionPolicy.description:
Owner-controlled toggles for what the PUBLIC projection may reveal.

Default-deny: a viewer sees only what these toggles explicitly allow,
on top of the always-public artifact (hero, narrative, chosen photos).
Exact dates are OFF by default; the destination headline is on (it's
the point of the share) but can be turned off.

RegisterDeviceRequest.title: RegisterDeviceRequest
RegisterDeviceRequest.description: Mobile client calls this on login / app start to register its push token.

RelatedDossierItem.title: RelatedDossierItem
RelatedDossierItem.description: Lightweight dossier stub for the Keep Reading carousel.

ResolveRequest.title: ResolveRequest
ResolveRequest.description:
Resolve (accept/reject) a change proposal.

The resolver is the authenticated actor — they must be an
organizer of the trip. No ``resolved_by`` in the body; trusting
the client to name the resolver would bypass authz.

ResultGroup.title: ResultGroup
ResultGroup.description:
The typed sections a result can land in. Each maps to a section header
in the overlay and to a destination surface when tapped.

Values marked DEFERRED are defined here so the frontend can build against
the final contract. ``RECEIPTS``, ``COSTS``, and ``ACTIONS`` now have
trip-scoped readers; ``ASK`` remains client-owned/deferred.

RevertRequest.title: RevertRequest
RevertRequest.description:
Revert an already-accepted proposal, restoring the prior itinerary state.

Only the trip organizer may revert. The ``resolution_note`` is stored on
the proposal row and surfaced in the group chat bridge message so members
understand why the change was undone.

``mode`` remains accepted for older clients. Canonical proposals always
commit the exact typed inverse; whole-version restoration is no longer a
product mutation path.

RouteRequest.title: RouteRequest
RouteRequest.description: Inputs to the modality routing decision.

SaveBoardRequest.title: SaveBoardRequest
SaveBoardRequest.description:
Body for ``POST /api/atlas/board/save``.

A kept board is addressed by the full query shape the engine composed:
``raw_text`` (if typed), ``facet_set`` (possibly empty for the whole-board
gift), and ``residual_phrase``. ``dimension``/``value`` remain as a
compatibility shortcut for older one-chip callers.

SaveBoardResponse.title: SaveBoardResponse
SaveBoardResponse.description:
The id of the saved (or revived) kept board. Saves are idempotent on
(user, facet_set), so a repeat save returns the same id.

SavedBoardCover.title: SavedBoardCover
SavedBoardCover.description:
Lightweight visual hint for a kept-board row.

The board itself is still never frozen: saved rows store the query that
re-composes live. This descriptor only lets the library render a small
thumbnail without recomposing every row.

SavedBoardCoverCrop.title: SavedBoardCoverCrop
SavedBoardCoverCrop.description: Normalized saliency crop for a saved-board cover thumbnail.

Scope.title: Scope
Scope.description:
The active search scope — drives which groups are searched and how
they are filtered. Mirrors the chip rail in the overlay.

``all_vesper``  — everything this user can see (default, no trip).
``places``      — public + saved places only.
``atlas``       — the user's Atlas memories only.
``nearby``      — places near the user's current location (home-city use).
``this_trip``   — scoped to ``trip_id`` (default when launched on a trip).
``receipts``    — bookings / reservations / receipts for the trip.
``costs``       — trip expenses / settlement shares.
``actions``     — non-executing Vesper action/audit rows for the trip.

SearchInterpretation.title: SearchInterpretation
SearchInterpretation.description:
Structured interpretation of a search query, generated by Vesper (Haiku).

``label`` is a 3-7 word natural-language restatement of the user's intent
(suitable for display above results).  ``mode`` distinguishes open-ended
vibes queries (``"intent"``) from specific lookup queries (``"lookup"``).
``entity_focus`` is the primary entity type the query targets.

city.title: City
city.description: Destination slug scope (e.g. 'lisbon'). Omit for global search.

experience_type.title: Experience Type
experience_type.description: concert, theater, comedy, festival, tour, etc.

entity_types.title: Entity Types
entity_types.description: Entity types to search: ["venue", "experience", "site", "accommodation", "angle", "dossier"]. "event" is a legacy alias for "experience". Defaults to all.

SearchRequest.title: SearchRequest
SearchRequest.description: Unified search request — drives the Discover tab search bar.

SearchResponse.title: SearchResponse
SearchResponse.description: Unified search response with fused results.

SearchResultItem.title: SearchResultItem
SearchResultItem.description: A single search result — venue, experience, site, or accommodation.

SerializedActionReceipt.title: SerializedActionReceipt
SerializedActionReceipt.description:
What the API returns after channel-based filtering.

private_influences are never present — only safe_label values are
surfaced via the public_reasons fallback mechanism.

SessionInfoResponse.title: SessionInfoResponse
SessionInfoResponse.description: Basic info about the current session.

SetPhoneRequest.title: SetPhoneRequest
SetPhoneRequest.description: E.164 format (e.g. +14155551234). Pass null to clear.

SettleBatchItem.title: SettleBatchItem
SettleBatchItem.description: One leg of a batch settlement — settle ``user_ids`` on ``expense_id``.

SettleBatchRequest.title: SettleBatchRequest
SettleBatchRequest.description:
Body for ``POST /api/trips/{trip_id}/settle-batch``.

Each item carries the same authorization contract as the single-expense
settle endpoint; the batch path simply replaces N round-trips with one
when the UI settles a payer's whole row at once.

SettleBatchResponse.title: SettleBatchResponse
SettleBatchResponse.description: All updated expenses, in request order.

SettlementPaymentView.title: SettlementPaymentView
SettlementPaymentView.description: Payment + display names, for the payments-history read surface.

ShareStats.title: ShareStats
ShareStats.description: Owner-facing share metrics. Aggregate counts only — never viewer identity.

ShareView.title: ShareView
ShareView.description: Owner-facing view of a share — what the app shows after publishing.

SituationAmbientNearbyCandidate.title: SituationAmbientNearbyCandidate
SituationAmbientNearbyCandidate.description: Backend-authored nearby suggestion for ambient trip surfaces.

SituationAttention.title: SituationAttention
SituationAttention.description: The top attention candidates derivable without running an LLM.

SituationGroup.title: SituationGroup
SituationGroup.description: Group context safe for shared/app surfaces.

SituationPlanBlock.title: SituationPlanBlock
SituationPlanBlock.description: Compact reference to the block most relevant right now.

SituationTimeline.title: SituationTimeline
SituationTimeline.description: Where the trip is on its timeline and what is next.

SituationVoiceSession.title: SituationVoiceSession
SituationVoiceSession.description: Public-safe summary of the active voice-guide arc.

SourceRef.title: SourceRef
SourceRef.description: A piece of evidence or system event that caused the action.

SpineReceipt.title: SpineReceipt
SpineReceipt.description: Spine — day-shape progress, not a completeness gauge (Standing floor).

StayCandidate.title: StayCandidate
StayCandidate.description: One saved stay option for a trip.

StayChosenResponse.title: StayChosenResponse
StayChosenResponse.description: Result of choosing a candidate — the stay it became.

StayDecisionResponse.title: StayDecisionResponse
StayDecisionResponse.description: Everything the compare/vote surfaces need in one read.

StayVoteState.title: StayVoteState
StayVoteState.description:
The resolved group-vote position.

``phase`` is the design's vote arc; ``leader_id`` is the front-runner (None
on a tie or before any vote); ``missing_voter_ids`` are members who haven't
voted yet. ``decided`` means a majority is locked OR everyone voted and one
candidate leads outright. ``blocking`` is the narrower "everyone voted, one
holdout" case — see ``resolve_stay_vote`` for the exact boundary with
``decided``. ``blocking_member_ids`` is only populated in that phase.

StayVoteTally.title: StayVoteTally
StayVoteTally.description: Per-candidate vote count + who voted (for the compare/vote modules).

StoryCorrection.title: StoryCorrection
StoryCorrection.description: Owner correction applied to a generated story section.

StorySharePreview.title: StorySharePreview
StorySharePreview.description:
Dry-run of a share before publishing (the Privacy Preview payload).

``public`` is exactly what a viewer would see; the counts/flags let the
UI explain the privacy state in plain language without re-implementing
the redaction rules.

StorySourceSignal.title: StorySourceSignal
StorySourceSignal.description: Human-readable provenance for a story section.

SuggestResponse.title: SuggestResponse
SuggestResponse.description: Autocomplete suggestions for the search bar.

TakeGenerationRequest.title: TakeGenerationRequest
TakeGenerationRequest.description: Body for POST /api/takes — request to generate (or fetch cached) a Take.

body.title: Body
body.description: Ordered list of content blocks. Paragraphs are the default; use headings only when the Take has 3+ distinct sections; use lists when enumerating 3+ items that read poorly as prose.

TakePayload.title: TakePayload
TakePayload.description:
The full Take content — what gets rendered (and what gets logged).

Tier 1 (Curator) and Tier 2 (Personal) share this shape. Personal Takes
include grounded `hypotheses` with real evidence refs; Curator Takes
leave `hypotheses` empty.

TakeResponse.title: TakeResponse
TakeResponse.description: Response shape for non-streaming Take requests.

TransportGap.title: TransportGap
TransportGap.description: One inter-city transition with no realistic transport plan.

TripBudgetUpdate.title: TripBudgetUpdate
TripBudgetUpdate.description:
Body for PATCH /api/trips/{trip_id}/budget.

All fields optional with no default — the route uses ``model_fields_set``
to distinguish "clear it" (``null``) from "not provided" (absent). A
field that the caller didn't include is left untouched on the trip.

TripContext.title: TripContext
TripContext.description: Trip-scoped context for filtering and relevance.

TripContextChangePreviewRequest.title: TripContextChangePreviewRequest
TripContextChangePreviewRequest.description: Proposed context values; omitted fields retain their current value.

TripContextDayMutation.title: TripContextDayMutation
TripContextDayMutation.description: One existing itinerary day re-anchored by a contextual replan.

TripContextMutation.title: TripContextMutation
TripContextMutation.description: Full proposed trip context plus the exact current-day remap.

message.title: Message
message.default: This trip already has a dated itinerary. Preview a contextual replan before changing its destination or dates.

TripContextReplanRequiredDetail.title: TripContextReplanRequiredDetail
TripContextReplanRequiredDetail.description: Typed explanation for context edits that cannot use raw trip PATCH.

TripDebriefSubmit.title: TripDebriefSubmit
TripDebriefSubmit.description: Structured post-trip debrief — funnels explicit traveler signal into memory.

TripDeparturePreflight.title: TripDeparturePreflight
TripDeparturePreflight.description:
Current facts a client must show before ending a membership.

This is deliberately a read model, not authorization for the later delete:
the removal transaction re-checks structural invariants and balances.
``requires_force`` is retained for wire compatibility and is always false;
all departures must settle costs first.

TripDigest.title: TripDigest
TripDigest.description:
A daily, trip-summary, or planning-context structured digest.

`content` is the LLM's structured JSON output; `rendered_markdown` is a
pre-formatted textual view populated at generation time so consumers
(planner prompt, admin UI) don't need to re-render the payload.

TripHero.title: TripHero
TripHero.description:
The typed hero payload attached to ``Trip.hero`` (not persisted).

``kind``/``severity`` are routing/register hints; ``identity``/``read``/
``receipt``/``step`` are the renderable content. Live/time-pressure states
(Orient/Urgent/Ready) override this from their own FE hooks rather than the
trips-list field.

phase.title: Phase
phase.description: Trip-status-relative phase for map UX: ideation | planning | final_prep | active | completed (from ``Trip.status`` via ``_TRIP_STATUS_TO_PHASE`` in the map-state builder). This answers “where is the trip in its lifecycle?” — distinct from ``HomeAnchor.phase`` on the trip-home feed, which is calendar-relative (ideation | pre | during | post) from ``compute_phase_info`` over today's date vs trip dates.

saved_nearby.title: Saved Nearby
saved_nearby.description: User-saved venues within ~5 km of the destination centroid (Layer 2). Empty when the trip has no destination or the user has no saves near it. Sourced from ``entity_saves`` JOIN ``venues`` via ST_DWithin.

neighborhoods.title: Neighborhoods
neighborhoods.description: Neighborhood/district polygons for the destination city, sourced from ``places`` rows with polygon geometry. Empty list when no destination is set or the destination has no child neighborhood rows. Used by the frontend to render a semi-transparent fill layer (Layer 3).

photos.title: Photos
photos.description: Geotagged trip photos with GPS coordinates (P3 photo layer). Empty when no photos have been uploaded and geotagged. Visibility-filtered: only group / group_and_learn photos are returned.

TripPhoto.title: TripPhoto
TripPhoto.description: One row from trip_photos as returned by the API.

TripPhotoBlockPatch.title: TripPhotoBlockPatch
TripPhotoBlockPatch.description: Body for PATCH /{photo_id}/block — manual block assignment / removal.

TripPresenceResponse.title: TripPresenceResponse
TripPresenceResponse.description:
Tier 0 group live-presence (docs/working/Chat Waiting Experience Plan.md,
Workstream A) — is anyone's turn with Vesper in flight on this trip right
now? Polled by other group members' chat screen so they can render
"{name} is asking Vesper…" without any new streaming/websocket infra.

TripStoryAtlasHandoff.title: TripStoryAtlasHandoff
TripStoryAtlasHandoff.description:
GET /api/trips/{trip_id}/story/handoff response.

A factual, trip-scoped receipt of what can feed Atlas. Saving a story,
sharing a story, and learning from photos are deliberately separate.

TripStoryAudio.title: TripStoryAudio
TripStoryAudio.description:
Metadata for Vesper's read-aloud of a Trip Story (homecoming v2).

Audio bytes are never carried in this model — they stream from the audio
endpoint. ``status`` is one of pending / ready / failed.

TripStoryGroupConsent.title: TripStoryGroupConsent
TripStoryGroupConsent.description:
Per-uploader photo-learning state for group trips.

This is a derived read model over ``trip_photos.visibility``. It is not a
new consent table: ``group_and_learn`` means included, ``group`` means the
uploader has not opted those photos into learning yet.

TripStoryHandoffSignal.title: TripStoryHandoffSignal
TripStoryHandoffSignal.description: One evidence/control row in the trip-scoped Atlas handoff receipt.

TripStoryPhotoSlot.title: TripStoryPhotoSlot
TripStoryPhotoSlot.description: Placeholder where a photo will land. photo_url is set when the user picks one.

TripStoryPhotoSlotEdit.title: TripStoryPhotoSlotEdit
TripStoryPhotoSlotEdit.description:
Body of a PATCH to a single photo slot.

``photo_state='none'`` means the user explicitly wants no photo here.
``photo_url=null`` without a state is the older clear-to-empty behavior.

TripStorySection.title: TripStorySection
TripStorySection.description: A single addressable narrative section of a Trip Story.

TripStorySectionCorrectionRequest.title: TripStorySectionCorrectionRequest
TripStorySectionCorrectionRequest.description: Body for owner correction actions on a story section.

TripStorySectionEdit.title: TripStorySectionEdit
TripStorySectionEdit.description: Body of a PATCH to a single section.

TripStorySectionProvenance.title: TripStorySectionProvenance
TripStorySectionProvenance.description: Owner-facing provenance and correction state for one section.

TripSummaryResponse.title: TripSummaryResponse
TripSummaryResponse.description: AI-generated retrospective for a completed trip.

UniversalSearchItem.title: UniversalSearchItem
UniversalSearchItem.description:
A single grouped result row.

``route`` is a client-resolvable destination hint (e.g. ``trip:<id>``,
``place:<slug>``, ``atlas:<id>``, ``conversation:<id>``) — the frontend
maps it to a navigation target. ``is_action`` marks command rows
(rendered with the Vesper spark, not a nav chevron).

trip_id.title: Trip Id
trip_id.description: Active trip UUID; required for trip scopes

lat.title: Lat
lat.description: User latitude; required for nearby scope

lng.title: Lng
lng.description: User longitude; required for nearby scope

UniversalSearchRequest.title: UniversalSearchRequest
UniversalSearchRequest.description:
A Universal Search query.

``trip_id`` is required when ``scope`` is ``this_trip``, ``receipts``,
``costs``, or ``actions``;
ignored otherwise.  ``lat`` / ``lng`` are required when ``scope`` is
``nearby`` — they set the centre of the geo-radius search over personal
Atlas memories.  ``per_group_limit`` caps each section so no single
group floods the overlay.

UniversalSearchResponse.title: UniversalSearchResponse
UniversalSearchResponse.description:
Grouped results for a query.

``groups`` appears in a FIXED order determined by ``scope`` (see
``SCOPE_GROUPS`` in dispatch.py) — groups are never reordered by
relevance, since retrieval sources use incommensurable score scales
(pg_trgm similarity vs Qdrant cosine/RRF) that can't be compared
meaningfully across groups. Within each group, ``items`` IS ordered
most-relevant-first by that group's own score. Empty groups are omitted.

Note: the overlay's elevated Ask-Vesper card is currently driven by a
client-side intent heuristic. A server-side ``interpretation`` field could
drive it instead — intentionally not part of this contract yet.

UpdateConversationAgencyRequest.title: UpdateConversationAgencyRequest
UpdateConversationAgencyRequest.description: Patch the group room's Vesper agency settings.

shared_plan_successor_user_id.title: Shared Plan Successor User Id
shared_plan_successor_user_id.description: Required when demoting the current shared-plan owner. Must name another current organizer; ownership transfer and demotion commit together.

source_url.title: Source Url
source_url.description: URL of the image to rehost.

UserFact.title: UserFact
UserFact.description:
A single structured fact about a traveler.

Supersession forms a linked list: a fact with ``valid_to`` set AND
``superseded_by`` pointing at a newer fact means "I now know better".
``valid_to`` set with ``superseded_by`` null means "retired without
replacement" (forget_fact).

UserFactsResponse.title: UserFactsResponse
UserFactsResponse.description: Active user facts grouped by namespace, plus the namespace catalog.

UserMapResponse.title: UserMapResponse
UserMapResponse.description: GET /api/me/map response — all mapped venues across all trips.

UserMapTrip.title: UserMapTrip
UserMapTrip.description:
One trip that contributed at least one mapped venue.

The frontend keys pin color off ``trip_id`` and looks up trip metadata
(title, dates, status) here, so the per-venue payload stays small.

UserMapVenue.title: UserMapVenue
UserMapVenue.description:
A single placed stop on the cross-trip map.

``block_id`` is the itinerary block id (stable per stop) — venues can
recur across trips, so the block id is the right de-dup/key handle.
Only blocks that resolved to real coordinates appear here.

UserMeResponse.title: UserMeResponse
UserMeResponse.description: Response for GET /api/me — full profile data for the Me tab.

UserMessageResponse.title: UserMessageResponse
UserMessageResponse.description: Response when a message is persisted without triggering an agent reply.

UserOpenProposalSummary.title: UserOpenProposalSummary
UserOpenProposalSummary.description: Open proposal row with trip context for the global unresolved bar.

UserUpdate.title: UserUpdate
UserUpdate.description: Partial update — only include fields you want to change.

VenueBriefItem.title: VenueBriefItem
VenueBriefItem.description: Compact venue brief — the 20% slot in the global Discover feed.

VenueDetailResponse.title: VenueDetailResponse
VenueDetailResponse.description:
Shape returned by GET /api/venues/{venue_id}.

Mirrors ``APIVenueDetail`` in the frontend types.

Verdict.title: Verdict
Verdict.description:
Opinion layer. Derived from signals, not free LLM judgment.

`derived_from` lists the signal categories the verdict is grounded in,
for traceability and debugging.

VisualSlot.title: VisualSlot
VisualSlot.description:
One image slot the engine fully resolves; the FE renders it verbatim and
never picks a material (see ``Atlas — Board Visual Elements.md`` → the
``visual_slots`` contract).

Pre-launch: every slot is ``source: "riso"`` with an ``asset_ref`` of the
form ``riso:<dimension>/<value>`` (e.g. ``riso:cuisine/pizza``,
``riso:category/viewpoint``); ``crop`` is null (riso is drawn to fit). The
``photo`` / ``img2img`` sources are production materials.

VoiceGuideSession.title: VoiceGuideSession
VoiceGuideSession.description:
One arc the user moved through, anchored at an opening entity.

A session opens when narration starts at a stop and stays open while
the user moves through related stops (same place, same angle ladder)
within a rolling window. ``arc_summary`` is the running text the next
narration call appends to and feeds back to the LLM. ``closed_at``
lands when a new place is entered or the user goes idle past a
threshold.

VoiceHandoffRequest.title: VoiceHandoffRequest
VoiceHandoffRequest.description: Request for an interrupted-narration voice session.

VoiceHandoffResponse.title: VoiceHandoffResponse
VoiceHandoffResponse.description: Token + context for opening an interrupted-narration voice session.

VoiceMetricsWindow.title: VoiceMetricsWindow
VoiceMetricsWindow.description:
Echoed-back window + sample size so the caller knows what they
actually got — important when the requested window had no data.

VoicePersonaInfo.title: VoicePersonaInfo
VoicePersonaInfo.description:
Persona metadata surfaced to the client so the UI can render the
persona name + voice provenance during a voice session.

Phase 4.6. The client uses this to show "Talking with Mateus" in the
voice overlay and to know which voice (id) the TTS will use. The
client doesn't need full persona spec — just identity for display.

VoiceTokenRequest.title: VoiceTokenRequest
VoiceTokenRequest.description:
Request a LiveKit access token for voice chat.

B1 audit fix: ``user_id`` and ``user_role`` are no longer accepted
from the request body — they're sourced from the authenticated
actor instead. Previously a missing-auth handler let anyone mint a
LiveKit JWT as any user for any trip by sending arbitrary body
fields. The request only carries display metadata now.

VoiceTokenResponse.title: VoiceTokenResponse
VoiceTokenResponse.description: LiveKit connection credentials for the client.

WeekRibbonReceipt.title: WeekRibbonReceipt
WeekRibbonReceipt.description: WeekRibbon — the lived week, one cell per day (Returned · Shape).

mime_type.title: Mime Type
mime_type.description: MIME type — Anthropic vision accepts JPEG, PNG, WebP, GIF.

backend__api__routes__chat__MessageImage.title: MessageImage
backend__api__routes__chat__MessageImage.description:
Inline image attached to a chat message.

The Anthropic chat API accepts image content blocks in messages —
we forward the base64 + mime type through the agent loop so the
concierge can see what the user is asking about ("what is this
monument?", "is this dish vegetarian?", scan a paper menu).

Sized for mobile: clients should compress to <=1MB base64 (typical
JPEG at 1920px wide, quality 0.7 = 150-300KB). Larger images flow
through the existing receipt-upload path instead.

Mirrors the receipt-OCR call shape (``backend/expenses/receipt_ocr.py``)
so the same vision-capable code path serves both surfaces.

location.description: Optional current GPS reading from the client. Written to conversation metadata before the turn runs; read by the concierge's distance tool when from_origin='current_location'.

images.title: Images
images.description: Optional images to attach to this message. The concierge answers questions about what's in the photo (translate a menu, identify a monument, scan an entry sign). Multiple images are concatenated in order. Limit 4 per message.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Optional structured per-turn metadata from the client (e.g. {'angle_id': 42} when entering from the angle detail screen). Persisted on the user message row and surfaced to the agent.

backend__api__routes__chat__SendMessageR.title: SendMessageRequest
backend__api__routes__chat__SendMessageR.description: A single message from a user to the concierge.

backend__api__routes__chat__SendMessageR.title: SendMessageResponse
backend__api__routes__chat__SendMessageR.description: What the concierge returns after processing a message.

mime_type.title: Mime Type
mime_type.description: MIME type accepted by the concierge vision path.

backend__api__routes__conversations__Mes.title: MessageImage
backend__api__routes__conversations__Mes.description: Inline image attached to a conversation-scoped Vesper turn.

trigger_agent.title: Trigger Agent
trigger_agent.description: If False, persist the message without triggering an agent reply. Useful for user-to-user chat in group conversations.

location.description: Optional current GPS reading from the client. Stored on the conversation's metadata before the turn runs.

images.title: Images
images.description: Optional images to attach to this turn. Mirrors the trip-scoped chat endpoint so private Vesper chats do not silently drop photo context.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Optional turn metadata from the client. Used for structured entry-point grounding such as metadata.conversation_seed.

retry_message_id.title: Retry Message Id
retry_message_id.description: Durable ID of this actor's original user message when resuming a failed or interrupted turn. The server validates ownership, conversation, and content before reusing its private idempotency key.

backend__api__routes__conversations__Sen.title: SendMessageRequest
backend__api__routes__conversations__Sen.description: Send a message in a conversation.

backend__api__routes__conversations__Sen.title: SendMessageResponse
backend__api__routes__conversations__Sen.description: Agent reply after processing a message.

backend__core__models__composition__Prov.title: Provenance
backend__core__models__composition__Prov.description:
Where one moment came from — the **Discover variant** trust signal.

The Atlas variant composes the traveler's OWN past, which is inherently
trusted, so it never populates provenance. The Discover variant composes the
WORLD corpus — venues/sites the engine is *pointing at*, not recalling — so
each moment carries who is vouching for it: ``source`` is the trust-ladder
rung (Vesper editorial / a dossier / a creator / a friend), ``byline`` is the
human attribution string, ``face`` an optional avatar/illustration ref.
``moment_id`` pins it to the matching ``Moment.id`` so the FE renders the
byline on the right card.

backend__core__models__universal_search_.title: Provenance
backend__core__models__universal_search_.description:
Where a result came from and how sensitive it is.

``label`` is the short, ALL-CAPS-in-UI string the row displays
(e.g. "FROM YOUR ATLAS", "YOUR TRIP", "SHARED BY ANA", "DISCOVER GUIDE").
``privacy`` gates rendering: a ``private`` row must always show its label.
